zh: 沟通——人类的连接——是个人和事业成功的关键。
en: Communication — the human connection — is the key to personal and career success.

zh: 知之者不如好之者，好之者不如乐之者。
en: Those who know it are not as those who love it; those who love it are not as those who find their joy in it.

zh: 我将为我的国家鞠躬尽瘁，死而后已。
en: I will work heart and soul for my country to the day I draw my last breath.

zh: 通过每天忠实地工作八小时，你最终可能成为老板，然后每天工作十二小时。
en: By working faithfully eight hours a day you may eventually get to be boss and work twelve hours a day.

zh: 大多数学生在学校生活早期会进行智商（智力商数）测试。
en: Most students do an IQ (Intelligence Quotient) test early in their school life.

zh: 即使他们从未看到结果，他们也觉得自己的智商决定了他们将在生活中表现如何。
en: Even if they never see their results, they feel that their IQ is what determines how well they are going to do in life.

zh: 当他们看到其他学生比自己做得更好时，他们通常认为那些学生有更高的智商，并且自己无法改变现状。
en: When they see other students doing better than them, they usually believe that those students have a higher IQ and that there is nothing they can do to change their situation.

zh: 然而，关于情商（情绪商数）的新研究表明，成功不仅仅是高智商的结果。
en: However, new research into EQ (Emotional Quotient) suggests that success is not simply the result of a high IQ.

zh: 虽然你的智商告诉你你有多聪明，但你的情商告诉你你如何运用你的智慧。
en: While your IQ tells you how intelligent you are, your EQ tells you how well you use your intelligence.

zh: 发明情商一词的萨洛维教授给出了以下描述：在工作中，是智商让你被雇佣，但却是情商让你获得晋升。
en: Professor Salovey, who invented the term EQ, gives the following description: at work, it is IQ that gets you employed, but it is EQ that gets you promoted.

zh: 根据他的研究，萨洛维教授提出，在预测某人未来的成功时，通过情商测试衡量的性格实际上可能比智商更重要。
en: Supported by his research, Professor Salovey suggests that when predicting someone's future success, their character, as measured by EQ tests, might actually matter more than their IQ.

zh: 萨洛维教授可能是正确的。
en: Professor Salovey may be correct.

zh: 例如，你是否曾想过为什么班上一些最聪明的学生，你认为他们应该取得好成绩，有时却考试不及格？
en: For example, have you ever wondered why some of the smartest students in your class, who you think deserve good grades, sometimes end up failing exams?

zh: 也许他们的失败是因为他们的情商较低。
en: Perhaps their failure is because of their lower EQ.

zh: 人们常常错误地认为高智商的人也总是拥有高情商。
en: People are often mistaken in thinking that those with high IQs always have high EQs as well.

zh: 这种关联可能存在，但同样可能的是，一个低智商的人拥有高情商，或者一个高智商的人拥有低情商。
en: This association can exist, but it is just as possible for someone with a low IQ to have a high EQ or someone with a high IQ to have a low EQ.

zh: 人们普遍认为，情商高的人对新思想持开放态度，并对生活抱有积极的态度。
en: It is generally believed that people with high EQs are open to new ideas and have positive attitudes towards life.

zh: 他们也不太可能被内心的问题所困扰。
en: They are also less likely to be troubled by internal problems.

zh: 另一方面，毫无疑问，情商低的人通常在与他人相处和处理困难情况方面存在问题；因此，他们在生活和职业适应上更加困难。
en: On the other hand, there is little doubt that people with low EQs often have problems getting on with other people and dealing with difficult situations; hence they have a harder time adjusting in life and in their careers.

zh: 人们普遍认为一个人的智商是由出生决定的。
en: People generally believe that a person's IQ is determined by birth.

zh: 然而，大多数社会科学家认为情商与教育有很大关系。
en: However, most social scientists agree that EQ has a lot to do with education.

zh: 一些人正在研究提高一个人情商的可能性，特别是在“人际技能”方面，比如理解和沟通。
en: Some are trying to study the possibility of improving a person's EQ, especially in terms of "people skills', such as understanding and communication.

zh: 梅耶教授被许多人公认为研究人情商变化的顶尖专家，他最近公布了一项关于高中生的研究结果。
en: Professor Mayer, recognised by many as a leading expert in the study of changes to people's EQs, recently announced the results of a study on senior secondary school students.

zh: 当学生们被介绍给那些有残疾的人时，他们发现，事后，非残疾学生更愿意帮助有困难的人。
en: When students were introduced to those who had disabilities, they found that, afterwards, the non-disabled students were more willing to help people with difficulties.

zh: 与未参与研究的学生相比，他们也表现出对残疾学生感受的更好理解。
en: Compared to students who had not been involved in the study, they also showed a better understanding of the disabled students' feelings.

zh: 残疾学生的态度也发生了明显的变化。
en: There was also an obvious change in the disabled students' attitudes.

zh: 他们对生活变得更加积极，更愿意尝试新事物。
en: They became more positive about life and more willing to try new things.

zh: 总之，研究结果表明，情商即使不比智商更重要，也至少同样重要。
en: Altogether, the results of studies show that EQ is as important, if not more important than IQ.

zh: 要在世界上取得成功并过上幸福成功的生活，需要与他人相处融洽，并能够以最佳方式理解和应对各种情况。
en: To get ahead in the world and lead a happy successful life requires getting on well with other people and being able to understand and react to situations in the best way possible.

zh: 高情商对此是必要的——越高越好。
en: A high EQ is necessary for this — the higher the better.

zh: 而情商有可能提高这一事实意味着，学校需要确保学生接受他们真正需要的教育，不仅仅基于智商，而是基于发展完整的品格。
en: And the fact that it might be possible to raise EQs means that schools need to make sure that their students are receiving an education they really need, not solely based on IQ but on developing the entire character.

zh: 发明了情商术语的萨洛维教授给出了以下描述：在工作中，智商让你被雇佣，但情商让你得到晋升。
en: Professor Salovey, who invented the term EQ, gives the following description: at work, it is IQ that gets you employed, but it is EQ that gets you promoted.

zh: 在他的研究支持下，萨洛维教授提出，在预测某人未来的成功时，通过情商测试衡量的性格实际上可能比智商更重要。
en: Supported by his research, Professor Salovey suggests that when predicting someone's future success, their character, as measured by EQ tests, might actually matter more than their IQ.

zh: 与未参与研究的学生相比，他们也表现出对残疾学生感受的更好理解。
en: Compared to students who had not been involved in the study, they also showed a better understanding of the disabled students' feelings.

zh: 他们无意追随父母的脚步，甚至不打算长期从事一份工作。
en: have no intention of following in their parents' footsteps or even staying at one job for very long.

zh: 事实上，计划在整个职业生涯中都在同一个领域或行业工作已经不再现实。
en: In fact, planning to work in the same field or industry for your entire working life just isn't practical anymore.

zh: 其中一个原因是技术。
en: One reason for this is technology.

zh: 你现在拥有的技能不太可能一直适用，也不足以支撑你整个职业生涯。
en: The skills you have now are unlikely to remain relevant and be enough to help you through your entire career.

zh: 事实上，它们可能很快就会过时。
en: In fact, they will probably be out of date very soon.

zh: 那你该怎么办？
en: And then what will you do?

zh: 这些策略可能会给你带来美好、舒适的生活：从事你喜欢的工作，赚取体面的薪水，并在你还年轻健康的时候退休。
en: These strategies might bring you a nice, comfortable life: working at a job you like, earning a decent salary and retiring while you're still young and healthy.

zh: 但我们今天大多数人必须超越“职业”这个小框框来看问题。
en: But most of us today have to look beyond the little box called "career".

zh: 如果你认为每天工作八小时就能建立职业生涯，那就再想想吧。
en: If you think you can work eight hours a day and build a career, think again.

zh: 如果你完成了要求你做的工作，你或许能保住你的工作，但这很难说是取得巨大职业成功的秘诀。
en: You might be able to keep your job if you finish what you are asked to do, but this is hardly a recipe for great career success.

zh: 相反，你最好不断寻找更聪明的方法来完成你的工作。
en: Instead, you'd better keep looking for smarter ways to do your work.

zh: 你需要成为一个风险承担者、创新者、问题解决者和努力工作的人。
en: You'll need to be a risk taker, an innovator, a problem solver, and a hard worker.

zh: 坚持这样做，有一天你就能成为某个领域的专家或专业人士。
en: Keep doing this, and you can become an expert or a specialist in something one day.

zh: 你有机会选择去建立一份职业生涯，而不是被告知留下或离开。
en: You have a chance to choose to build a career, rather than being told to stay or to go.

zh: 如今，工作和职业以惊人的速度出现和消失。
en: Jobs and occupations come and go at an amazing pace these days.

zh: 如果你认为你不可能被软件取代，或者你的工作不可能外包给月球上的某些机器人，那你就错了。
en: If you think you can't be replaced by software, or your job can't be outsourced to some robots on the moon, you are wrong.

zh: 如果你在这个领域工作了10年后，你的工作消失了怎么办？
en: What if your job disappears after working for 10 years in the field?

zh: 你可能不得不回到教室接受再培训，以获得在另一个领域工作的资格。
en: You may have to go back to the classroom and be retrained in order to qualify to work in another field.

zh: 事实上，你很可能将不得不多次这样做。
en: In fact, in all likelihood, you will have to do this multiple times.

zh: 但你可以在被淘汰出职业生涯之前，为发展和变化做好准备。
en: But you can be prepared for developments and changes before you are thrown out of a career.

zh: 是主动学习，还是被迫学习，这完全是你自己的选择。
en: It is entirely your choice to learn, or to be forced to learn.

zh: 简而言之，如果你想在21世纪的职业生涯中取得成功，你就必须掌握新的必备技能。
en: In short, if you want to succeed in a 21st century career, you will have to develop new essential skills.

zh: 从一个雇主那里获得终身保障不再确定，甚至不太可能。
en: Lifetime security from one employer is no longer certain or even likely.

zh: 事实是，你是自己职业生涯的老板，你能做什么以及能做得多好，取决于你自己。
en: The truth is that you are the boss of your career, and it is up to you to decide what you can do and how well you can do it.

zh: 你准备好了吗？
en: Are you ready for this?

zh: 我写信是为了申请《学生邮报》的一个职位，我是在上周的校报上看到招聘广告的。
en: I am writing to apply for a position at Student Post, which I saw advertised in the school newspaper last week.

zh: 我对本地新闻记者的职位很感兴趣。
en: I would be interested in the role of reporter for local events.

zh: 随信附上我的简历副本。
en: I enclose a copy of my CV.

zh: 我今年17岁，在第五高级中学读二年级。
en: I am 17 years old and I am in my second year at No. 5 Senior Secondary School.

zh: 我想为您工作，因为我对成为一名记者非常感兴趣。
en: I would like to work for you because I am very interested in becoming a journalist.

zh: 我也想更多地了解城市里发生的事情，因为我觉得了解我们居住的城市很重要。
en: I would also like to learn more about the things that are going on in the city, as I feel that it is important to know about the city we live in.

zh: 我是一个勤奋且尽责的人。
en: I am a hard-working and committed person.

zh: 我参加了一个志愿者项目，在过去三年里探望我所在地区的老年人。
en: I have participated in a volunteer programme, visiting elderly people in my area for the last three years.

zh: 此外，我觉得我能与来自不同背景的人相处融洽，因为我曾与许多不同的人一起为本地活动做过很多项目。
en: Moreover, I feel that I can get on well with people from different backgrounds since I have worked with lots of different people on many projects for local events.

zh: 我认为我具备成为一名优秀记者的语言技能。
en: I think I have the language skills to be a good reporter.

zh: 我一直擅长写作，并且阅读过许多小说。
en: I have always been good at writing and I have read many novels.

zh: 去年，我在我们的区写作比赛中获得了二等奖。
en: Last year, I won second prize in our District Writing Competition.

zh: 此外，我也是一名优秀的摄影师，这可能对撰写某些文章有所帮助。
en: In addition, I am also a good photographer, which might be useful for some of the articles.

zh: 我期待您的回复。
en: I look forward to hearing from you.

zh: 也许，你是一个聪明的人，拥有丰富的知识和创造性的想法，但当你搬到一个新环境或必须与一些新人一起工作时，他们并不总能立即认可你或尊重你。
en: Perhaps, you are a smart person with good knowledge and creative ideas, but when you move to a new environment or have to work with some new people, it is not always easy for them to recognise you or respect you immediately.

zh: 你如何与他人相处？
en: How can you get along with others?

zh: 你如何让其他人关注你并尊重你？
en: How can you get other people to pay attention to you and respect you?

zh: 保持好奇心，愿意学习新事物、新知识和新技能。
en: Be curious and be willing to learn new things, knowledge and skills.

zh: 搬到一个新的工作环境，你可能需要处理不属于你自己领域的事情。
en: Moving to a new working environment, you may need to deal with things that don't belong to your own field.

zh: 在这种情况下，更愿意学习新事物的人可以变得更加灵活，能够满足工作中的新要求。
en: In such a case, people who are more willing to learn new things can become more flexible and be able to meet new requirements in their job.

zh: 在重要方面要有良好的判断力。
en: Have a good sense of judgement on important aspects.

zh: 在一个新环境中学习或工作，你在参加讨论或做决定时需要具有良好的判断力。
en: Learning or working in a new environment, you need to have a good sense of judgement when joining a discussion or making a decision.

zh: 你应该考虑什么是真正正确的，并着眼于长期目标，不要被短期的细小选择所干扰。
en: You should consider what is virtually right, and look for long-term goals, and not be distracted by small choices for the short term.

zh: 与人交谈时要自信并进行眼神交流。
en: Be confident and make eye contact when talking to people.

zh: 当你外出与人会面时，显得自信是很重要的。
en: When you go out and meet people, it is important to look confident.

zh: 例如，如果你要去参加面试，但你不自信，你能做什么？
en: For example, if you are going to an interview, but you don't feel confident, what can you do?

zh: 在你走进去之前，对自己说：“我掌控这个房间。”
en: Before you walk in, think to yourself, "I own the room."

zh: 然后，昂首挺胸，微笑着走进去。
en: Then, walk in holding your head high and smile.

zh: 记住要直视他人的眼睛——这是与人建立联系的关键。
en: Remember to look other people in the eye — it's the key to creating a connection with people.

zh: 很快，你就会感觉和你看起来一样自信。
en: Soon, you will feel as confident as you look.

zh: 设定明确的目标并学习。
en: Set clear goals and learn.

zh: 当与他人一起工作时，拥有一个清晰的愿景和实现它的工作计划是很重要的。
en: When working together with others, it is important that you have a clear vision and a working plan to achieve it.

zh: 让人们知道你代表什么。
en: Let people know what you stand for.

zh: 你需要找出哪些想法能够真正团结人们，然后清晰地表达这些想法。
en: You need to find out which ideas can really unite people and then express those ideas clearly.

zh: 同时，要尊重地平等对待你的团队成员。
en: Also, treat your team members respectfully as equals.

zh: 工作时，不要专注于哪些是你的工作，哪些不是。
en: When working, don't focus on what is your work and what isn't.

zh: 做完自己的工作后，要随时准备帮助他人。
en: After doing your own work, always be ready to help others.

zh: 上午9点至下午5点：大多数人的标准工作日。
en: 9 am — 5 pm: the standard working day for most people.

zh: 然而，对其他人来说，这是就寝时间。
en: However, for other people, it's bedtime.

zh: 这些人是夜班工作者——上夜班的护士、穿越大陆的卡车司机和准备早餐面包的面包师。
en: These people are the night workers — nurses on night shift, lorry drivers crossing continents and bakers preparing breakfast bread.

zh: 虽然在这个时间工作可能有一些好处，但夜间工作也带来了特殊的问题：家庭生活困难，社交生活有限。
en: While there may be some advantages to work at this time, working at night also brings particular problems: family life is difficult and social life is limited.

zh: 此外，咖啡馆和餐馆都关门了。
en: Furthermore, cafés and restaurants are closed.

zh: 交通选择很少，而且对健康有害。
en: There are few transportation options and it's bad for health.

zh: 尽管有这么多问题，到底谁会选择上夜班呢？他们打算继续做下去吗？
en: With all these problems, who would choose to work on the night shift anyway, and are they planning to stay in their jobs?

zh: 我晚上工作是因为我对星星着迷，从我小时候起就一直如此。
en: I work at night because I'm fascinated by the stars, and I have been since I was a little boy.

zh: 当我通过望远镜观察时，我是最快乐的。
en: I'm happiest when I'm looking down my telescope.

zh: 星星令人惊叹，给它们拍照很棒。
en: The stars are amazing, and it's great taking pictures of them.

zh: 说实话，我永远不会换工作。
en: Honestly, I'm never going to change my job.

zh: 我打算一辈子做天体摄影师。
en: I'm going to be an astro-photographer for life.

zh: 当然，我的社交生活很差，但我想我已经找到了解决这个问题的办法。
en: Of course, my social life is poor, but I think I've found a solution to this problem.

zh: 我打算带着我的笔记本电脑去工作，并加入一个在线聊天室。
en: I'm going to bring my laptop to work and join a chat room online.

zh: 我希望能和国外的人交朋友。
en: I'm hoping to make friends with people abroad.

zh: 他们是我工作时唯一醒着的人！
en: They're the only people awake when I'm working!

zh: 我的工作涉及修理铁轨，所以我们必须在晚上工作，因为白天他们不能停止火车运行。
en: My work involves repairing the railway tracks, so we have to work at night because they can't stop the trains during the day.

zh: 我们的团队精神很好，而且我们还有额外的报酬。
en: Our team spirit is great, and we get paid extra.

zh: 然而，我很快就会离开这份工作。
en: However, I'm going to leave this job soon.

zh: 这很累人，而且我必须找一份正常的工作，因为我妻子怀孕了，我想在晚上陪在她和我的孩子身边支持他们。
en: It's tiring and I have to find a normal job because my wife's pregnant and I want to be around in the evenings to support her and my baby.

zh: 我还没有找到新工作，但我希望成为一名建筑工人。
en: I haven't found a new job yet, but I'm hoping to be a builder.

zh: 这是份辛苦的工作，但不用再上夜班了！
en: It's hard work, but no more nights!

zh: 当我来到这个国家时，我唯一能找到的工作是夜班保安。
en: When I came to this country, the only work I could find was as a night security guard.

zh: 这份工作很无聊，因为太安静了，但我决定把这个问题变成优势。
en: This job is boring because it's so quiet, but I've decided to turn this problem into an advantage.

zh: 下个月，我将通过远程学习开始攻读文学学位。
en: Next month, I'm going to start a degree in literature by distance learning.

zh: 我将在漫长安静的夜晚阅读我的课程书籍！
en: I'm going to read my course books during the long quiet nights!

zh: 我不想一辈子当保安。
en: I don't want to be a security guard all my life.

zh: 我想成为一名教师。
en: I'd like to be a teacher.

zh: 等我完成学位后，我打算申请一个教师培训课程。
en: I'm going to apply for a teacher-training course when I finish my degree.

zh: 我真正想做的是与高中生一起工作，帮助他们规划未来。
en: What I'd really like to do is working with high school students and helping them with their future.

zh: 我在印度的一个呼叫中心工作，但我接听来自英国人的电话，所以由于印度和英国之间的时差，我不得不在晚上工作。
en: I work in a call centre in India, but I answer calls from people in the UK so I have to work at night because of the time difference between India and the UK.

zh: 这是一份薪水不错的好工作。
en: It's a good job with a good salary.

zh: 这也很有趣，因为我可以使用我的英语。
en: It's also interesting because I can use my English.

zh: 但是，说实话，晚上工作很令人沮丧——我想念我的朋友和家人——所以我想很快离开这份工作。
en: But, to be honest, working at night is depressing — I miss my friends and family — so I'd like to leave this job soon.

zh: 然而，高薪工作不容易找到，所以我真的不知道我该怎么办。
en: However, well-paid jobs aren't easy to find, so I don't know what I'm going to do, really.

zh: 嗯，如果我找不到新工作，我想我可以留在这份工作里，多存点钱。
en: Well, if I can't find a new job, I guess I can stay in this one and save more money.

zh: 我仍然可以在周末见到我的家人和朋友。
en: I can still see my family and friends at the weekend.

zh: 直到最近，人们还认为智商是衡量智力最重要的标准，那些高智商的人才是生活中能取得成功的人。
en: Up until quite recently, it was believed that IQ was the most important measure of intelligence and those with high IQs were the ones who get ahead in life.

zh: 然而，专家们现在表示，情商即使不是更重要，也同样是预测一个人在生活中是否会成功的重要因素。
en: However, experts now say that EQ is as important if not more in predicting whether someone will be successful in life.

zh: 发明这个术语的专家萨洛维教授解释说，在职场上，智商让你被雇佣，但情商让你获得晋升。
en: Professor Salovey, the expert who invented the term, explains that in the workplace IQ gets you employed but EQ gets you promoted.

zh: 高情商的人被描述为快乐、善于交际，并且能够很好地处理他们生活和事业的各个方面。
en: People with high EQs are described as happy, sociable and can deal with well in all areas of their lives and careers.

zh: 他们能够管理自己的情绪，并且不太可能被负面的内心感受和想法所困扰。
en: They can manage their emotions and are less likely to be troubled by negative internal feelings and thoughts.

zh: 那些低情商的人难以应对挑战和困境；因此压力更大，也更不快乐。
en: Those with low EQs struggle to adjust challenges and difficult situations; hence are more stressed and unhappy.

zh: 智商和情商之间可能存在关联，但并不总是如此。
en: There can be an association between IQ and EQ but not always.

zh: 高智商的人可能情商不高，反之亦然。
en: People who have high IQs may not be emotionally intelligent and vice versa.

zh: 例如，如果那些学业成绩优异且聪明的学生未能在考试中获得他们应得的成绩，这可能是因为他们的情商较低。
en: For example, if students who are highly academic and intelligent fail to achieve the grades they deserve in exams it may be because they have lower EQs.

zh: 我最近和薛女士谈到了我们未来几年需要的技能以及工作和职业的未来趋势。
en: I recently spoke to Ms Xue about the skills we will need in years to come and the future trend for jobs and careers.

zh: 据薛女士说，未来许多常规的办公室和工厂工作将会消失。
en: According to Ms Xue, in the future a lot of routine office and factory jobs will disappear.

zh: 我们需要拥有许多我们现在就可以开始培养的技能。
en: We need to have many skills which we can start developing now.

zh: 其中最重要的一项是批判性思维，即分析和评估信息的能力。
en: One of the most important is critical thinking, which is the ability to analyse and evaluate information.

zh: 逻辑思维，即良好运用我们推理的能力，将在职场中非常有用，因为它将帮助我们运用知识去说服他人。
en: Logical thinking, which is the ability to use our reason well, will be highly useful in the workplace, as it will help us use our knowledge to persuade others.

zh: 理解能力将受到高度重视，公司总是会欣赏一个可靠、可以信任其完成工作的人。
en: Comprehension skill will be highly valued and companies will always appreciate a reliable person who they can trust to do their job.

zh: 即使在工作场所之外，我们也将需要一些重要的技能。
en: Even outside the workplace, there are important skills we will need.

zh: 例如，当我们开始赚钱并承担财务责任时，良好的理财技能将使我们受益匪浅。
en: For example, good financial skill will benefit us greatly when we start earning money and have financial responsibilities.

zh: 我最喜欢阅读文学作品，比如小说、诗歌、短篇故事和民间故事。
en: I like reading literary works the most, such as novels, poetry, short stories, and folk tales.

zh: 我觉得侦探小说引人入胜且令人兴奋。
en: I find detective stories fascinating and exciting.

zh: 我总是欣赏写作的逻辑。
en: I always enjoy the logic of the writing.

zh: 我喜欢其中的谜团。
en: I love the mysteries in them.

zh: 我大声朗读并享受其中的描述。
en: I read aloud and enjoy the description.

zh: 我背诵故事中我最喜欢的部分。
en: I recite my favourite parts of the story.

zh: 我尝试找出矛盾的信息并自己解开谜题。
en: I try to figure out contradictory messages and solve the riddles myself.

zh: 它有一个好的情节，而且角色很有趣！
en: It has a good plot, and the character is interesting!

zh: 伟大的文学不过是语言承载意义到最大可能的程度。
en: Great literature is simply language charged with meaning to the utmost possible degree.

zh: 诗歌不过是最美丽、最感人、最广泛有效的表达方式。
en: Poetry is simply the most beautiful, impressive, and widely effective mode of saying things.

zh: 文学是人类最有趣和最重要的表达方式之一。
en: Literature is one of the most interesting and significant expressions of humanity.

zh: 文学和蝴蝶是人类已知的两种最甜蜜的激情。
en: Literature and butterflies are the two sweetest passions known to man.

zh: 格林尼治村是艺术人士聚集的地方，他们寻找着朝北窗户且租金低廉的公寓。
en: Greenwich Village is a place where the art people came together, hunting for apartments with north-facing windows and low rents.

zh: 在一栋三层楼的顶层，苏和约翰西拥有她们的工作室。
en: At the top of a three-story building, Sue and Johnsy had their studio.

zh: 十一月，一个冰冷的、看不见的陌生人——医生们称之为肺炎——来到了格林尼治村。
en: In November, a cold, unseen stranger, whom the doctors called Pneumonia, arrived in Greenwich Village.

zh: 肺炎先生把他冰冷的手指放在了约翰西身上；她躺在床上，几乎一动不动，盯着毯子下空白的墙壁。
en: Mr Pneumonia placed his icy finger on Johnsy; and she lay, barely moving, in her bed staring at a blank wall under her blanket.

zh: 医生把苏拉到一边，声称：“她只有十分之一的机会——让我们这么说吧。
en: The doctor took Sue aside and claimed, "She has one chance in — let us say, ten.

zh: 而那个机会就在于她自己想要活下去。
en: And that chance is for her to want to live.

zh: 试着鼓励她去想一些更充满希望的事情，关于未来，关于生活。”
en: Try to encourage her to think more hopeful thoughts, about the future, about living."

zh: 苏拿了一些纸和一支笔进了约翰西的房间，开始用钢笔画画，试图提振约翰西的精神。
en: Sue took some paper and a pen into Johnsy's room and began a pen drawing, in an attempt to raise Johnsy's spirits.

zh: 约翰西的眼睛是睁开的，但它们并没有看着她。
en: Johnsy's eyes were open, but they weren't watching her.

zh: 相反，她凝视着窗外，倒数着：“十，九，八，七......”
en: Instead, she stared out the window and counted backwards: "Ten, nine, eight, seven ..."

zh: “你在数什么，亲爱的？”苏问道。
en: "What are you counting, dear?" asked Sue.

zh: “它们现在掉得更快了，”约翰西回答。
en: "They're falling faster now," replied Johnsy.

zh: “三天前还有将近一百片。
en: "Three days ago there were almost a hundred.

zh: 现在只剩下五片了。
en: There are only five left now.

zh: 常春藤上还有五片叶子。
en: Five leaves on the ivy vine.

zh: 当最后一片叶子落下时，我也必须走了。
en: When the last one falls, I must go, too.

zh: 医生没告诉你吗？”
en: Didn't the doctor tell you?"

zh: “哦，我从没听过这种胡话，”苏迅速回答道。
en: "Oh, I never heard of such nonsense," quickly replied Sue.

zh: 但约翰西的心意已决。
en: But Johnsy's mind was made up.

zh: “我想看最后一片叶子落下。
en: "I want to see the last one fall.

zh: 我想飘落下去，飘下去，就像那些可怜又疲倦的叶子一样。”
en: I want to go sailing down, down, just like one of those poor, tired leaves."

zh: “试着睡吧，”苏说。
en: "Try to sleep," said Sue.

zh: 然后她去请了她的邻居，贝尔曼先生，来当她绘画的模特。
en: Then she went to fetch her neighbor, Mr Behrman, to be a model for her drawing.

zh: 他是个失败的画家。
en: He was a failed painter.

zh: 40年来，他从未画出他渴望创作的那幅杰作。
en: In 40 years he had never produced the masterpiece he longed to paint.

zh: 苏告诉他约翰西想像叶子一样悄然离去的渴望。
en: Sue told him about Johnsy's longing to slip away like the leaves.

zh: “世界上竟有这种傻瓜，因为藤上的叶子掉了就觉得自己会死？
en: "Are there people in the world that foolish to think they'll die because leaves drop off from a vine?

zh: 这不是让约翰西小姐病倒的地方。
en: This is not a place in which Miss Johnsy shall lie sick.

zh: 总有一天我会画出一幅杰作，然后我们都会离开。”
en: Someday I will paint a masterpiece, and we shall all go away."

zh: 贝尔曼和苏望向窗外的藤蔓，注意到只剩下一片叶子了。
en: Behrman and Sue looked out the window at the vine and noticed that there was just one remaining leaf.

zh: 更甚的是，天正下着冷雨，还夹杂着雪。
en: Whats more, a cold rain was falling, mixed with snow.

zh: 第二天早上，约翰西醒来，要求拉开窗帘。
en: The next morning, Johnsy woke and asked for the curtains to be opened.

zh: 一片叶子仍留在藤上，在风雨中勇敢地坚持着。
en: One leaf remained on the vine, bravely holding on in the wind and rain.

zh: 而我会和它一起死。”
en: And I will die with it."

zh: 又过了一天，约翰西再次要求拉开窗帘。
en: The following day, Johnsy asked again for the curtains to be opened.

zh: 那片叶子还在那里！
en: The leaf was still there!

zh: 约翰西躺着看了它很久。
en: Johnsy lay for a long time looking at it.

zh: “我一直是个坏女孩，苏，”约翰西说。
en: "I've been a bad girl, Sue," said Johnsy.

zh: “渴望死亡是一种罪过。”
en: "It is a crime to want to die."

zh: 然后她要了一些食物。
en: Then she asked for some food.

zh: 下午，医生来探望，“有五成希望，”医生说，“有好的护理，你会赢的。
en: In the afternoon, the doctor visited, "Even chances," said the doctor, "with good nursing, you'll win.

zh: 现在我得去看看楼下的一个人，贝尔曼。
en: And now I must see a fellow downstairs, Behrman.

zh: 他没有希望了。”
en: There is no hope for him."

zh: 第二天，苏来到约翰西的床边。
en: The next day, Sue came to Johnsy's bed.

zh: “我有事要告诉你，”她说。
en: "I have something to tell you," she said.

zh: 他只病了两天。
en: He was ill for only two days.

zh: 昨天早上管理员发现他痛苦无助地躺在床上。
en: The janitor found him lying in bed helplessly with pain yesterday morning.

zh: 他的鞋子和衣服都湿透了，冰冷刺骨。
en: His shoes and clothing were wet through and icy cold.

zh: 他们无法想象在那样一个可怕的夜晚他去了哪里。
en: They couldn't imagine where he had been on such a terrible night.

zh: 然后他们发现了一个灯笼，还亮着，还有一些画笔，亲爱的，看看窗外，看墙上最后的那片常春藤叶子。
en: And then they found a lantern, still lit, and some brushes, and look out the window, dear, at the last ivy leaf on the wall.

zh: 你难道不奇怪为什么风吹时它一动也不动吗？
en: Didn't you wonder why it never moved an inch when the wind blew?

zh: 啊，亲爱的，那是贝尔曼的杰作——他在最后一片叶子落下的那晚把它画在了那里。”
en: Ah, dear, it's Behrman's masterpiece — he painted it there the night when the last leaf fell."

zh: 这是谁的树林我想我知道。
en: Whose woods these are I think I know.

zh: 他的房子在村子里；
en: His house is in the village;

zh: 他不会看见我停在这里
en: He will not see me stopping here

zh: 看着他的树林积满雪。
en: To watch his woods fill up with snow.

zh: 我的小马一定觉得奇怪
en: My little horse must think it queer

zh: 停在附近没有农舍的地方
en: To stop without a farmhouse near

zh: 在树林和冰封的湖之间
en: Between the woods and frozen lake

zh: 一年中最黑的夜晚。
en: The darkest evening of the year.

zh: 他摇了摇他的马具铃铛
en: He gives his harness bells a shake

zh: 询问是否有什么错误。
en: To ask if there is some mistake.

zh: 唯一其他的声音是轻拂
en: The only other sound's the sweep

zh: 轻柔的风和绒毛般的雪花。
en: Of easy wind and downy flake.

zh: 树林可爱、幽暗而深邃，
en: The woods are lovely, dark and deep,

zh: 但我有诺言要遵守，
en: But I have promises to keep,

zh: 入睡前还有数英里要走，
en: And miles to go before I sleep,

zh: 入睡前还有数英里要走。
en: And miles to go before I sleep.

zh: 我孤独地漫游，像一朵云
en: I wandered lonely as a cloud

zh: 高高地飘浮在山谷和小山之上，
en: That floats on high o'er vales and hills,

zh: 突然间我看到一群，
en: When all at once I saw a crowd,

zh: 一大片，金黄色的水仙花；
en: A host, of golden daffodils;

zh: 在湖边，在树下，
en: Beside the lake, beneath the trees,

zh: 在微风中飘动和舞蹈。
en: Fluttering and dancing in the breeze.

zh: 像繁星一样连绵不断，
en: Continuous as the stars that shine,

zh: 在银河中闪烁，
en: And twinkle on the milky way,

zh: 它们延伸成一条永无止境的线
en: They stretched in never-ending line

zh: 沿着海湾的边缘：
en: Along the margin of a bay:

zh: 我一眼望去，看到成千上万，
en: Ten thousand saw I at a glance,

zh: 欢快地舞动着它们的头。
en: Tossing their heads in sprightly dance.

zh: 它们旁边的波浪也在舞蹈；但它们
en: The waves beside them danced; but they

zh: 在欢乐中胜过闪闪发光的波浪：
en: Outdid the sparkling waves in glee:

zh: 诗人不能不感到快乐，
en: A poet could not but be gay,

zh: 在如此欢乐的陪伴中：
en: In such a jocund company:

zh: 我凝视着——凝视着——但很少想到
en: I gazed—and gazed—but little thought

zh: 这景象给我带来了怎样的财富：
en: What wealth the show to me had brought:

zh: 因为常常，当我躺在沙发上
en: For oft, when on my couch I lie

zh: 处于空虚或沉思的情绪中，
en: In vacant or in pensive mood,

zh: 它们闪现在内心的眼睛上
en: They flash upon that inward eye

zh: 那是孤独的极乐；
en: Which is the bliss of solitude;

zh: 然后我的心充满了快乐，
en: And then my heart with pleasure fills,

zh: 并与水仙花一起舞蹈。
en: And dances with the daffodils.

zh: 撞击如此猛烈，以至于我对从船上跌落没有清晰的记忆。
en: The crash was so violent that I have no clear recollection of falling from the boat.

zh: 我还能指望得救吗？
en: Might I hope to be saved?

zh: 我看到一个黑色的物体在东方消失。
en: I saw a black mass disappearing in the east.

zh: 我呼喊着，绝望地朝船游去。
en: I shouted, swimming towards the boat in desperation.

zh: 我的嘴里灌满了水；我挣扎着不被拉得更深、更深。
en: My mouth filled with water; I struggled against being drawn deeper, deeper down.

zh: 突然，我感到自己被迅速拉到了海面；我听到这些话：“如果主人能好心抓住我的肩膀，主人游泳就会轻松得多。”
en: Suddenly, I felt myself quickly drawn up to the surface of the sea; and I heard these words: "If master would be so good as to hold onto my shoulder, master would swim with much greater ease."

zh: 我用一只手抓住了我忠实的康塞尔的胳膊。
en: I grabbed my faithful Conseil's arm with one hand.

zh: “是你吗？”我说。
en: "Is it you?" said I.

zh: “那次撞击把你和我都抛进了海里？”
en: "That crash threw you as well as me into the sea?"

zh: “不；但是，既然我是为主人服务的，我就跟着他。”
en: "No; but, being in my master's service, I followed him."

zh: 康塞尔冷静而镇定。
en: Conseil was cool and calm.

zh: 然而，我们的处境同样可怕。
en: Nevertheless, our situation was no less terrible.

zh: 将近凌晨一点钟，我突然感到非常疲惫。
en: Near one o'clock in the morning, I suddenly felt terribly tired.

zh: 康塞尔不得不支撑着我，我们的生存全靠他一个人了。
en: Conseil had to keep me up, and our preservation depended on him alone.

zh: “离开我！离开我！”我对他说。
en: "Leave me! Leave me!" I said to him.

zh: “绝不！”他回答。“我宁愿先淹死。”
en: "Never!" replied he. "I would drown first."

zh: 在我看来，似乎有一个喊声回应了他。
en: It seemed to me as if a cry answered him.

zh: 一个人的声音回应了我们的声音！
en: A human voice responded to ours!

zh: 然后，不知为何，那个奇怪、可怕的动物的想法第一次出现在我的脑海里！
en: Then, I know not why, the thought of the strange, terrible animal came into my head for the first time!

zh: 我几乎没有任何力气了；我的手指几乎无法移动；我的嘴一张一合，灌满了咸水。
en: I had almost no strength left; my fingers could hardly move; my mouth, opening and closing, filled with salt water.

zh: 我变得越来越冷。
en: I became colder and colder.

zh: 我最后一次抬起头；然后我沉了下去。
en: I raised my head for the last time; then I sank.

zh: 就在这时，一个坚硬的物体撞击了我。
en: At this moment a hard body struck me.

zh: 我紧紧抓住它；然后我感觉自己被拉了起来，被带到了水面上，我的胸口塌陷了——我闭上眼睛，失去了知觉。
en: I held onto it; then I felt that I was being drawn up, that I was brought to the surface of the water, that my chest collapsed — my eyes closed, unconscious.

zh: 我很快醒了过来，半睁开眼睛。
en: I soon woke up, and half opened my eyes.

zh: 我看到了一张我立刻认出的脸。
en: I saw a face which I immediately recognised.

zh: “是我，先生！”那个加拿大人回答道。
en: "The same, sir!" replied the Canadian.

zh: “船撞毁的时候，你被抛到海里了吗？”
en: "Were you thrown into the sea when the boat crashed?"

zh: “是的，教授，但比您幸运，我几乎立刻就找到了安全的地方，就在海里的这个岛上。
en: "Yes, Professor, but more fortunate than you, I was able to find safety almost immediately upon this island in the sea.

zh: 或者，更准确地说，是在这头大鲸鱼上。
en: Or, more correctly speaking, on this large whale.

zh: 只是我很快就发现为什么我的武器没有刺入它的皮肤，而是弹了回来。”
en: Only I soon found out why my weapon had not entered its skin, but bounced off."

zh: “因为那个生物是钢铁做的。”
en: "Because that creature is made of steel."

zh: 我迅速移动到这个生物的顶部，踢了它一脚。
en: I moved myself quickly to the top of the being, and kicked it.

zh: 它显然是一个坚硬、坚固的物体。
en: It was evidently a hard, solid body.

zh: 支撑着我的黑色背部光滑、抛光过，一点也不像动物。
en: The black back that supported me was smooth, polished, nothing like an animal.

zh: 这一击产生了金属的声音；而且，尽管这可能难以置信，但它似乎是由金属板制成的。
en: The blow produced a sound of metal; and, incredible though it may be, it seemed as if it was made of metal plates.

zh: 这个怪物是人造的。
en: This monster was a human construction.

zh: 我们正躺在一艘某种水下船的背上。
en: We were lying upon the back of a sort of underwater boat.

zh: 我在外部到处寻找开口，一个洞，但什么也没找到。
en: I searched all over the outside for an opening, a hole, but found nothing.

zh: 终于，这个漫长的夜晚过去了。
en: At last this long night passed.

zh: 突然，从船内部传来一声噪音。
en: Suddenly a noise came from inside the boat.

zh: 一块铁板被移开了，一个人出现了，发出了一声奇怪的叫喊，然后立刻消失了。
en: One iron plate was moved, a man appeared, uttered an odd cry, and disappeared immediately.

zh: 片刻之后，八个强壮的男人，他们的脸被遮住了，悄无声息地出现，并把我们拉进了他们那不可思议的机器里。
en: Some moments after, eight strong men, their faces covered, appeared noiselessly, and drew us down into their incredible machine.

zh: 我们现在在机器上，我在黑暗中冷得发抖。
en: We were aboard the machine now, and | shook with cold in the darkness.

zh: 我的眼睛什么也看不见。
en: My eyes could see nothing.

zh: 我说不出我在哪里，也无法想象。
en: | couldn't say where | was, nor could | imagine.

zh: 一片漆黑，令我惊讶的是，即使过了几分钟，我的眼睛也看不到哪怕是最小的形状。
en: All was black, and | was amazed that even after some minutes, my eyes had not been able to see even the smallest shape.

zh: 我能听到内德和康塞尔急促的呼吸声。
en: | could hear Ned and Conseil breathing quickly.

zh: 我们的牢房突然亮了起来，我们在灯光下痛苦地闭上了眼睛。
en: Our prison was suddenly lit, and we closed our eyes painfully at the light.

zh: 接着传来一扇沉重的门打开的声音，两名水手出现了。
en: Then came the sound of a heavy door opening, and two seamen appeared.

zh: 他们非常仔细地检查了我们，一言不发。
en: They examined us with great attention, without saying a word.

zh: 然后他们又关上门离开了。
en: Then they shut the door again and left.

zh: “我们会死在这个监狱里的！”内德喊道。
en: "We will die in this prison!" cried Ned.

zh: “我们经历过比这更糟的情况都活下来了！”我并不完全相信自己说的话，但我知道这些话会安慰内德和康塞尔。
en: "We have survived worse than this!" | did not completely believe the words | said, but | knew they would comfort Ned and Conseil.

zh: 门又开了，那些人带着水和干饼干回来了。
en: The door opened again, and the men returned with water and dry biscuits.

zh: 这几乎算不上是一顿大餐，但它让我意识到我有多饿。
en: Hardly a large meal, but it made me realise just how hungry | was.

zh: “明天，我们带你们回家，”其中一个人用低沉的声音说。
en: "Tomorrow, we take you home," said one of the men in a deep voice.

zh: “但首先，你们必须告诉我们你们是如何找到我们的机器的。”
en: "But first, you must tell us how you found our machine."

zh: 我再也不会把我的家视为理所当然了。
en: | would never again take my home for granted.

zh: 我能听到内德和康塞尔急促的呼吸声。
en: I could hear Ned and Conseil breathing quickly.

zh: 我们的牢房突然亮了起来，我们在灯光下痛苦地闭上了眼睛。
en: Our prison was suddenly lit, and we closed our eyes painfully at the light.

zh: 他们非常仔细地检查了我们，一言不发。
en: They examined us with great attention, without saying a word.

zh: 我并不完全相信自己所说的话......
en: I did not completely believe the words I said...

zh: 其中一个男人用低沉的声音......
en: One of the men in a deep voice...

zh: 浪漫主义是18世纪末至19世纪中叶欧洲的一场文学和艺术运动。
en: Romanticism was a literary and artistic movement from the late 18th century to the mid-19th century in Europe.

zh: 浪漫的情感在诗歌、故事、艺术和音乐中得以表达。
en: Romantic emotions were expressed in poems, stories, art and music.

zh: 这些情感作品颂扬了自然之美和真实的人类情感。
en: These emotional works celebrated the beauty of nature and true human emotions.

zh: 威廉·华兹华斯是英国著名的浪漫主义诗人。
en: William Wordsworth was a famous Romantic poet in England.

zh: 事实上，他的名字与英国浪漫主义运动是同义词。
en: In fact, his name is synonymous with the English Romantic Movement.

zh: 华兹华斯在英格兰最美丽的地区之一长大，该地区被称为湖区。
en: Wordsworth grew up in one of the most beautiful areas of England, which is called the Lake District.

zh: 那里的乡村是野生的、自然的和美丽的。
en: The countryside there is wild, natural and beautiful.

zh: 华兹华斯的童年极大地影响了他后来的诗歌。
en: Wordsworth's childhood greatly influenced his later poetry.

zh: 成年后，华兹华斯与同行诗人塞缪尔·泰勒·柯勒律治合作。
en: As an adult, Wordsworth worked together with the fellow poet, Samuel Taylor Coleridge.

zh: 1798年，他们出版了一本名为《抒情歌谣集》的诗集。
en: In 1798, they published a book of poetry called Lyrical Ballads.

zh: 这本书宣告了英国浪漫主义运动的开始。
en: This book announced the beginning of the English Romantic Movement.

zh: 其中的诗歌使用了来自现实生活的语言和情境，并以一种新的方式颂扬自然。
en: Its poems used language and situations from real life, and celebrated nature in a new way.

zh: 这本书是对过去诗歌的巨大改变。
en: The book was a great change from the poetry of the past.

zh: 几个世纪以来，浪漫主义一直是全世界人类创造性表达的一部分。
en: Romanticism has been a part of human creative expression for centuries all over the world.

zh: 像中国最伟大的诗人之一李白，在唐朝期间写作诗歌，大约比华兹华斯早一千年。
en: Like Li Bai, one of China's greatest poets, wrote poetry during the Tang Dynasty, around a thousand years before Wordsworth.

zh: 他自由、生动和夸张的诗歌风格在许多方面是19世纪欧洲浪漫主义运动的早期形式。
en: His free, vivid and exaggerated style of poetry is in many ways an early form of the Romantic Movement in 19th century Europe.

zh: 与华兹华斯及其同时代人一样，李白的诗歌是关于美丽的自然景色和触动他心灵的人们。
en: As with Wordsworth and his contemporaries, Li Bai's poems were about beautiful natural scenes and the people who touched his heart.

zh: 小时候，李白和家人住在四川。
en: As a boy, Li Bai lived in Sichuan with his family.

zh: 25岁时，他开始周游全国。
en: At the age of 25, he began travelling around the country.

zh: 他与学者交朋友并记日记。
en: He made friends with scholars and kept journals.

zh: 他的旅行是他创作2000多首诗歌的基础，其中大约1800首至今仍被阅读。
en: His travels were the basis for his works of more than 2,000 poems, of which approximately 1,800 are still read today.

zh: 克劳迪斯国王是哈姆雷特的叔叔。
en: King Claudius is Hamlet's uncle.

zh: 他杀死了哈姆雷特的父亲并娶了哈姆雷特的母亲格特鲁德。
en: He killed Hamlet's father and married Hamlet's mother Gertrude.

zh: 克劳迪斯决定组织一场哈姆雷特和莱尔提斯之间的击剑比赛。
en: Claudius decides to organise a fencing match between Hamlet and Laertes.

zh: 莱尔提斯是被哈姆雷特意外杀死的波洛尼厄斯的儿子，也是奥菲莉亚的哥哥，奥菲莉亚是哈姆雷特的前女友，因哈姆雷特对她残忍而自杀。
en: Laertes is the son of Polonius who Hamlet accidentally killed and the brother of Ophelia, Hamlet's ex-girlfriend who killed herself because Hamlet was cruel to her.

zh: 克劳迪斯希望哈姆雷特被杀，以保护他作为丹麦国王的地位。
en: Claudius wants Hamlet to be killed in order to protect his position as King of Denmark.

zh: 他说服了想为父亲之死复仇的莱尔提斯，在比赛中使用一把毒剑来杀死哈姆雷特。
en: He convinces Laertes, who wants revenge for his father's death to use a poisoned sword in order to kill Hamlet during their match.

zh: 在战斗之前，哈姆雷特与他的好朋友霍拉旭交谈，然后克劳迪斯与格特鲁德、莱尔提斯以及宫廷中的其他人一起进入。
en: Before the fight, Hamlet talks with his good friend Horatio and then Claudius enters with Gertrude, Laertes and others from the royal court.

zh: 过来，哈姆雷特，和莱尔提斯握手。
en: Come Hamlet and shake hands with Laertes.

zh: 我为给你带来的痛苦以及我的恶劣行为感到抱歉。
en: I'm sorry for the pain I have caused you and for my terrible actions.

zh: 这其中是有原因的；一个我相信你已经听说过的原因。
en: There is a reason for it; one which I am sure you have heard.

zh: 是我的疯狂让我表现得如此糟糕。
en: It is my insanity that has made me act so badly.

zh: 我很感激你这么说——尽管我应该为你对我父亲所做的一切而复仇。
en: I appreciate you saying that - even though I should want revenge for what you have done to my father.

zh: 尽管我个人原谅你，但为了我家族的荣誉，我不能接受你的道歉。
en: Even though I forgive you personally, for the sake of the honour of my family I cannot accept your apology.

zh: 那么好吧，让我们进行这场击剑比赛吧。
en: Well then, let's play this fencing match.

zh: 奥斯里克，把他们的剑递给他们。
en: Osric, pass them their swords.

zh: 由于哈姆雷特是较弱的击剑手，莱尔提斯需要比他多击中三次才能赢得比赛。
en: Since Hamlet is the weaker fencer, Laertes will need three more hits than him to win the match.

zh: 让我们摆出一些酒杯。
en: Let's put out some wine glasses.

zh: 如果莱尔提斯击中第一或第二剑，我将假装为他的健康和好运祝酒，然后我会在他的酒里下毒。
en: If Laertes makes the first or second hit, I will pretend to drink to his health and good luck and I'll poison his wine.

zh: 我相信那是一次击中。
en: I believe that was a hit.

zh: 让我们都为哈姆雷特干杯。
en: Let's all drink to Hamlet.

zh: 哈姆雷特，拿着这杯酒和我一起喝。
en: Here Hamlet, take this glass of wine and drink with me.

zh: 我更愿意继续比赛。
en: I would prefer to keep playing.

zh: 是的，你击中我了。
en: Yes, you got me there.

zh: 我认为哈姆雷特会赢。
en: I think Hamlet is going to win this.

zh: 不，现在看看他。
en: No, look at him now.

zh: 哈姆雷特我亲爱的儿子，休息一下，用我的手帕擦擦你的额头。
en: Hamlet my dear son, rest for a minute and take my handkerchief to wipe your brow.

zh: 王后为你的健康、幸福和好运干杯。
en: The queen drinks to your health, happiness and good luck.

zh: 格特鲁德，把杯子放下！
en: Gertrude, put the glass down!

zh: 你不能告诉我是否可以喝酒！
en: You cannot tell me whether or not I can drink!

zh: 克劳狄斯：（自言自语）那是毒酒，但现在我无能为力。
en: Claudius: (to himself) That was the poisoned wine but there is nothing I can do about it now.

zh: 哈姆雷特：雷欧提斯，你是要击剑还是只是在闹着玩？来吧！准备好接第三剑！
en: Hamlet: Laertes, are you going to fence or are you just playing around? Come on! Get ready for the third hit!

zh: 雷欧提斯：你以为我只是在玩吗？那好，让我们真正地打一场！
en: Laertes: You think I'm only playing? Well then and let's really fight!

zh: 在接下来的片刻，雷欧提斯刺伤了哈姆雷特。
en: In the next moments, Laertes wounds Hamlet.

zh: 然后在打斗中，他们俩都掉了剑，并捡起了对方的剑。
en: Then in the fight they both lose their swords and pick up each other's.

zh: 哈姆雷特刺伤了雷欧提斯，与此同时，葛簇特倒下了。
en: Hamlet wounds Laertes and while this is happening, Gertrude collapses.

zh: 哈姆雷特：葛簇特王后怎么了？她不舒服吗？
en: Hamlet: What is the matter with Queen Gertrude? Is she unwell?

zh: 克劳狄斯：她只是受不了看到血。她刚刚晕倒了。
en: Claudius: She simply cannot stand the sight of blood. She has just fainted.

zh: 葛簇特：不，那是谎言！那杯酒有毒。哈姆雷特，我要死了！
en: Gertrude: No, that is a lie! The drink was poisoned. Hamlet, I'm dying!

zh: 哈姆雷特：谁对她做了这件事？
en: Hamlet: Who did this to her?

zh: 雷欧提斯：我有一部分责任，哈姆雷特。我在我们比赛前给我的剑涂了毒，你很快就会死。既然毒药已经进入你的血液，就没有解药了。
en: Laertes: I am partly at fault, Hamlet. I poisoned my sword before our match and you are going to die soon, Now that the poison is in your blood, there is no cure.

zh: 然而，我没有伤害你的母亲。
en: I did not harm your mother, however.

zh: 是国王在你母亲喝的酒里下了毒。
en: It was the King who poisoned the wine your mother drank.

zh: 哈姆雷特：你给剑涂了毒？那好，让毒药快点发作，杀了我吧！
en: Hamlet: You poisoned the sword? Well then hurry up poison and kill me!

zh: 哈姆雷特攻击克劳狄斯并用剑刺伤了他。
en: Hamlet attacks Claudius and wounds him with the sword.

zh: 哈姆雷特：给你，你这个杀人犯，现在你可以去陪我母亲了！喝下这杯酒。
en: Hamlet: Here, you murderer, now you can follow my mother! Drink this wine.

zh: 雷欧提斯：你做得对，哈姆雷特。国王罪有应得。
en: Laertes: You did the right thing, Hamlet. The King got what he deserved.

zh: 请原谅我所做的一切。
en: Please forgive me for everything that has happened.

zh: （雷欧提斯中毒而死。）
en: (Laertes dies from the poison.)

zh: 哈姆雷特：（对霍拉旭说）上帝会免除他的罪责。
en: Hamlet: (to Horatio) God will free him from blame.

zh: 我很快就会在天堂与他相会。
en: I'll be joining him in Heaven soon.

zh: 请把这里发生的一切真相告诉所有人。
en: Please tell everyone the truth about what has happened here.

zh: （他们听到军队逼近的声音。）
en: (They hear the sound of an army approaching.)

zh: 奥斯里克：哈姆雷特，年轻的福丁布拉斯在波兰赢得了战争，正凯旋而归。
en: Osric: Hamlet, young Fortinbras has won the war in Poland and is returning in triumph.

zh: 哈姆雷特：霍拉旭，我活不久了。
en: Hamlet: Horatio, I haven't long to live.

zh: 毒药正在杀死我。
en: The poison is killing me.

zh: 请告诉福丁布拉斯，我希望他成为丹麦的下一任国王。
en: Please tell Fortinbras that I want him to be the next King of Denmark.

zh: 务必让他知道这一点。
en: Make sure he knows this.

zh: 霍拉旭：现在一颗高贵的心正在破碎。
en: Horatio: Now a noble heart is breaking.

zh: 《最后一片叶子》是欧·亨利写的一篇美丽的短篇小说。
en: "The Last Leaf" is a beautiful short story written by O. Henry.

zh: 在故事中，两个主要角色，约翰西和苏，住在一栋三层楼的公寓工作室里。
en: In the story, the two main characters, Johnsy and Sue, lived in a studio apartment in a three-story building.

zh: 作者将疾病“肺炎”描述为一个人，他将冰冷的手指放在约翰西身上，使她病得很重。
en: The author describes the illness "pneumonia" as a person who placed an icy finger on Johnsy making her terribly ill.

zh: 她躺在床上，盖着毯子，茫然地看着墙壁。
en: She lay in bed under a blanket looking blank at the wall.

zh: 医生把苏拉到一边，告诉她约翰西生存的机会很小。
en: The doctor took Sue aside and told her that Johnsy had only a small chance of surviving the illness.

zh: 苏注意到约翰西并不是在盯着墙，而是在看着窗外常春藤上的叶子，并倒数着。
en: Sue noticed that Johnsy wasn't staring at the wall but was actually looking out the window at leaves on an ivy vine and counting backwards.

zh: 她说叶子已经落了几天了，当最后一片叶子落下时，她就会死。
en: She said they had been falling for days and when the last one fell, she would die.

zh: 苏去找她的邻居贝尔曼先生，告诉他约翰西说的关于想像叶子一样溜走的话。
en: Sue went to fetch her neighbor Mr Behrman and told him what Johnsy said about wanting to slip away like the leaves.

zh: 贝尔曼先生说那是胡说八道。
en: Mr Behrman said that was nonsense.

zh: 第二天早上，约翰西让苏拉开窗帘，发现尽管雨雪下了一整夜，最后一片叶子仍然留在藤上。
en: The next morning, Johnsy asked Sue to open the curtain and found that the last leaf remained on the vine, despite the rain and snow having fallen all night.

zh: 约翰西开始意识到她愚蠢的想法，并开始变得更健康。
en: Johnsy came to realise her foolish thought and started to become healthier.

zh: 然而，贝尔曼先生死于肺炎。
en: However, Mr Behrman died from pneumonia.

zh: 在一个雨雪交加的夜晚，他画了他的杰作——约翰西窗外墙上的一片藤叶。
en: In a snowing and raining night, he painted his masterpiece — a vine leaf on the wall outside of Johnsy's window.

zh: 这些天我没有太多时间阅读，但当我有一点时间时，我总是会从书架上拿一本科幻书。
en: I don't get so much time to read these days, but I will always grab a science fiction book off the shelf when I have a little time.

zh: 这是一个多么有趣的类型！
en: It's such a fun genre!

zh: 我目前正在读一本叫做《第三种选择》的书。
en: I'm currently reading a book called The Third Option.

zh: 这本书的背景设定在未来，当时世界正与另一个星球经历一场长期的战争。
en: The book is set in the future at a time when the world is experiencing a long and war with another planet.

zh: 另一方正试图寻找一个新的星球居住，并决定地球将成为他们的。
en: The other are trying to find a new planet to live on and have decided that the Earth will be theirs.

zh: 由于比我们更先进，他们不想要和平，而是要摧毁我们。
en: Being more advanced than us, they do not want peace but rather to destroy us.

zh: 为了赢得战争，人类决定开发一种非常致命的武器，它将杀死敌人，但也会破坏地球的环境。
en: Ina attempt to win the war, humans decide to develop a very deadly that will kill the enemy but also destroy the environment of the Earth.

zh: 这本书的主角是一位名叫凯特·琼斯的科学家，她认为地球的重要性太大了。
en: The main character of the book is a scientist called Kate Jones who decides that the of the Earth is too important.

zh: 她召集了一群士兵和科学家，共同寻找替代的解决方案。
en: She gathers a group of soldiers and scientists together to find an alternative solution.

zh: 他们称自己的小组为“，因为他们相信他们可以开启人类历史的新篇章。
en: They call their group "as they believe they could start a new chapter in human history.

zh: 当武器正在开发中时，他们与时间赛跑，以求生存并保护地球。
en: While the weapon is under they race against time to stay alive and protect the Earth.

zh: 我不想说太多而剧透，但如果你喜欢这种类型，我强烈推荐这本书！
en: I don't want to spoil it by saying too much but I highly recommend this book if you enjoy this genre!

zh: 当她迷失在森林中孤独地徘徊时，风在树木间呻吟。
en: The wind moaned through the trees as she wandered lost and lonely through the forest.

zh: 它像没有月亮或星星的漫长冬夜一样黑暗。
en: It was as dark as a long winter night without moon or stars.

zh: 她的恐惧像海洋中的波浪一样升起。
en: Her fear rose like waves in the ocean.

zh: 她的呼吸在冰冷的空气中变成了冰柱。
en: Her breath was icicles in the freezing air.

zh: 突然，森林中升起了一个影子。
en: Suddenly, through the forest rose a shadow.

zh: 黑暗、危险且比最高的山还大，它向她爬来。
en: Dark, dangerous and larger than the highest mountain, it crept towards her.

zh: 那是深夜。
en: It was late at night.

zh: 林赛独自一人在乡间走回家。
en: Lindsay was walking home alone in the countryside.

zh: 天太黑了，她几乎看不见任何东西。
en: It was so dark that she could hardly see a thing.

zh: 突然，她听到身后传来一声低沉的嚎叫。
en: Suddenly, she heard a low howl behind her.

zh: “也许是一只狼，”她心想。
en: "Perhaps it is a wolf," she thought to herself.

zh: 她开始走得更快，但发现在黑暗中很难看清自己要去哪里。
en: She began to walk more quickly, but found it difficult to see where she was going in the darkness.

zh: 她向前迈了一步，发现自己从山上摔了下来，翻滚了一次又一次。
en: She took a step forward, and found herself falling down a hill, rolling over and over.

zh: 当她终于停下来时，她全身疼痛。
en: When she finally stopped, her whole body was in pain.

zh: 她意识到她的腿可能断了。
en: She realised her leg was probably broken.

zh: 在摔倒的过程中，她掉了手机。
en: During the fall, she had dropped her phone.

zh: 林赛突然意识到她需要寻求帮助，而且要快。
en: Lindsay suddenly realised she needed to get help, and quickly.

zh: 她试图爬回山上拿她的手机。
en: She tried to climb back up the hill to her phone.

zh: 但这太困难也太痛苦了，她只移动了一米左右就放弃了。
en: But it was so difficult and so painful that she only moved a metre or so before she gave up.

zh: 接着嚎叫声又传来了。
en: Then came the howl again.

zh: 林赛再次试图爬回山上，但摔倒在地，无法移动她的腿。
en: Lindsay tried to climb back up the hill again, but fell to the ground, unable to move her leg.

zh: 突然，她感到脖子后面有呼吸。
en: Suddenly, she felt breath on the back of her neck.

zh: 人体能够做很多事情，例如跑得非常快、搬运重物、跳得很高、优美地跳舞、做体操、跑马拉松。
en: The human body is capable of doing many things, such as running very fast, carrying heavy things, jumping high, dancing beautifully, doing gymnastics, running marathons.

zh: 人类思维能够做决定、推理、论证、记忆事实、预测未来、思考并做出判断、推断意义。
en: The human mind is capable of making decisions, reasoning, justifying, memorising facts, predicting the future, thinking and making judgements, inferring meaning.

zh: 我们运用我们的思维去创造、创新、解决问题、计算。
en: We use our minds to create, innovate, solve problems, calculate.

zh: 我真的很钦佩那些运用他们的思维为人类谋福祉的人。
en: I really admire people who use their minds for the betterment of mankind.

zh: 例如，物理学家、医学研究人员、信息技术专家进行研究以发现未知的世界和宇宙，寻找疾病的治疗方法，制造新的发明以改善人类生活，创造机器人来为人类工作。
en: For example, physicists, medical researchers, information technologists conduct research to discover the unknown world and the universe, find cures for diseases, make new inventions to better human life, create robots to do the work for human beings.

zh: 人的生命是有限的，可是为人民服务是无限的。
en: A person's life is limited but serving the people is limitless.

zh: 我要把有限的生命，投入到无限的为人民服务之中去。
en: I want to devote my limited life to serving the people limitlessly.

zh: 健康才是真正的财富，而不是金银财宝。
en: Health that is real wealth and not pieces of gold and silver.

zh: 人体是人类灵魂最好的图画。
en: The human body is the best picture of the human soul.

zh: 每个人的大脑都是不同的——大脑使每个人变得独特，并定义了他或她是谁。
en: Every human brain is different — the brain makes each human unique and defines who he or she is.

zh: 克隆是制造植物或动物精确副本并使其自然或人工发育的过程。
en: Cloning is the process of making an exact copy of a plant or animal and developing it either naturally or artificially.

zh: 自然克隆已经持续了数代。
en: Natural cloning has been going on for generations.

zh: 例如，园丁从植物上取下插枝并将其放入合适的介质（如土壤）中时，他们就在使用一种克隆形式。
en: For example, gardeners have been using a form of cloning when they take a cutting from a plant and place it in a suitable medium, such as soil.

zh: 自然克隆的另一个例子是同卵双胞胎，他们是由同一个卵子产生的。
en: Another example of natural cloning is identical twins, who are produced from the same egg.

zh: 多年来，人们一直尝试人工克隆动物。
en: For years, there had been attempts to clone animals artificially.

zh: 第一个成功克隆的动物是1996年一只名叫多莉的羊。
en: The first successfully cloned animal was a sheep named Dolly in 1996.

zh: 起初，多莉生长正常，但后来她患上了一种通常在更年老的动物身上才发现的疾病。
en: At first, Dolly grew normally, but later she developed an illness that is more normally found in much older animals.

zh: 她活了六年半，仅是她被克隆的那只羊寿命的一半。
en: She lived for six and a half years, only half the life of the sheep from which she was cloned.

zh: 在多莉之后，科学家们已经克隆了超过20种哺乳动物，包括骆驼、牛、鹿、狗、山羊和小鼠。
en: After Dolly, scientists have cloned more than 20 mammal species, including camels, cattle, deer, dogs, goats and mice.

zh: 然而，他们在这些其他物种中也发现了类似的健康问题模式。
en: However, they have found a similar pattern of health problems with these other species as well.

zh: 这引发了一些问题，例如：“这对所有克隆动物来说会是一个主要问题吗？”“它会永远持续发生吗？”
en: This has led to questions such as, "Will this be a major issue for all cloned animals?" "Will it continue to happen forever?"

zh: 2018年1月，中国科学家宣布克隆了两只长尾猴，中中和华华。
en: In January 2018, the cloning of two long-tailed monkeys, Zhong Zhong and Hua Hua, was announced by Chinese scientists.

zh: 它们是有史以来第一批被克隆的灵长类动物（包括猿、猴和人类的哺乳动物目）！
en: They were the first-ever primates (the order of mammals which include apes, monkeys, and humans) to have been cloned!

zh: 与其他物种相比，克隆灵长类动物被证明“困难得多”，而用非生殖细胞进行克隆则更加复杂。
en: In comparison with other species, cloning primates has proved to be "much harder", and doing so with non-reproductive cells was even more complicated.

zh: 研究团队主任孙强博士说，他们全身心投入这项研究，并且每周7天、每天24小时地照顾着1000多只猴子，持续了5年多。
en: Dr. Sun Qiang, director of the research team, said they had been wholly devoted to the research and had been taking care of more than 1,000 monkeys 24 hours a day, 7 days a week for more than 5 years.

zh: 在他们最终找到成功克隆猴子的方法之前，曾经历过多次失败。
en: There had been a number of failures before they eventually found a way to successfully clone a monkey.

zh: 灵长类动物的成功克隆赢得了全球赞誉，被认为是一个巨大的突破，可能带来各种疾病的治愈方法，也可能为防止衰老过程提供线索。
en: The successful cloning of primates has led to worldwide praise as a huge breakthrough that might lead to cures for various diseases and may also offer clues on how to prevent the aging process.

zh: 借助这项技术，我们有可能在短时间内培育出大量具有相同基因的猴子，甚至可以通过改变它们的基因来适应研究需求。
en: With this technology, we have the potential to raise a large number of monkeys with identical genes in a short amount of time, and we can even change their genes to suit research needs.

zh: 这有助于节省研究时间，减少测试所需的动物数量，产生更准确的结果，并带来更有效的治疗方法。
en: This could help save research time, reduce the number of animals required for testing, produce more accurate results, and lead to more effective treatments.

zh: 然而，克隆仍然是一个引起大量分歧并激起强烈情绪的问题。
en: Cloning, however, continues to be an issue that causes a great deal of disagreement and provokes strong emotions.

zh: 虽然有些人认为它对科学进步至关重要，但另一些人则提出了道德上的担忧。
en: While some people believe it is crucial for scientific advancement, others raise moral concerns.

zh: 他们相信这势必会导致其他物种的克隆，并担心由此引发的伦理问题。
en: They believe it is bound to lead to cloning of other species, and they worry about the ethical questions this raises.

zh: 例如，他们想知道克隆体应该被视为物体，还是应该被视为具有自己身份的个体。
en: For example, they wonder if clones should be treated as objects or as individuals with their own identity.

zh: 此外，克隆体可能被视为仅仅是原件的复制品，因此被认为是低等的。
en: Furthermore, clones may be viewed as mere copies of originals thus being seen as inferior.

zh: 最后，他们担心一些组织可能为了不道德的目的而滥用这项技术。
en: Finally, they worry that some organisations may abuse the technology for unethical purposes.

zh: 到目前为止，中中和华华长大后会发生什么仍是未知数。
en: So far, it is still unknown what will happen to Zhong Zhong and Hua Hua as they grow up.

zh: 研究人员坚持认为，克隆科学应该被进一步探索和发展。
en: Researchers insist that the science of cloning should be further explored and advanced.

zh: 正如爱因斯坦所说：“科学是一种强大的工具。
en: As Einstein said, "Science is a powerful instrument.

zh: 它如何被使用，对人类是福是祸，取决于人类自己，而不是工具。
en: How it is used, whether it is a blessing or a curse to mankind, depends on mankind and not on the instrument.

zh: 刀是有用的，但它也能杀人。”
en: A knife is useful, but it can also kill."

zh: 流行病是指一种传染性疾病，在短时间内——通常是两周或更短——迅速传播给大量人群。
en: An epidemic is the name given to an infectious disease that rapidly spreads to a large number of people within a short period of time — usually two weeks or less.

zh: 自人类在地球上生活以来，流行病就一直在发生。
en: Epidemics have been happening for as long as there have been people living on Earth.

zh: 它们可能由多种不同因素引起，例如病毒被带入某个地区，或者人们生活方式的改变，比如更多人居住在一个地区，这增加了他们接触病毒或细菌的机会。
en: They can be caused by several different factors, such as a virus being carried into an area, or changes in the way people live, like more people living in an area, which increases the chances of them coming into contact with a virus or bacteria.

zh: 只有当某种疾病导致一定数量的死亡发生后，它才能被正式归类为流行病。
en: Each disease can only officially be classified as an epidemic once a certain number of deaths have occurred from the disease.

zh: 每种疾病的死亡人数是不同的。
en: The number of deaths is different for every disease.

zh: 因此，一种非常罕见的疾病将有一个较低的基线，在一个地方仅出现少数病例就会被归类为流行病；这与更常见的疾病（如流感）相反，后者具有较高的基线。
en: So a very rare disease will have a low baseline and just a few cases of it in one place will be classified as an epidemic; as opposed to more common diseases, such as the flu, that have a higher baseline.

zh: 普通感冒是一种影响数百万人的广泛传播的病毒，但它不被认为是一种足够严重的状况，值得被归类为流行病。
en: The common cold is a widespread virus that affects millions of people, but it is not seen as being a serious enough condition to deserve the classification of epidemic.

zh: 随着最近的新闻报道将禽流感与过去的致命流行病相提并论，吉姆·史密斯审视了有时令人沮丧的流行病历史，以寻找线索，了解我们如何能创造一个没有流行病的更积极的未来。
en: With recent press reports drawing parallels between bird flu and killer epidemics of the past, Jim Smith looks at the sometimes depressing history of epidemics for clues about how we can create a more positive future without epidemics.

zh: 自禽流感于1997年首次出现以来，它已夺走了一百多人的生命。
en: Since bird flu first appeared in 1997, it has taken more than a hundred lives.

zh: 许多人担心它可能成为下一次全球流行病，特别是因为那些详细研究了死于1918年西班牙流感者尸体组织样本的科学家已经证明，禽流感是该病毒的现代变种。
en: Many people fear it could be the next global epidemic, especially as scientists who have gone into detail studying tissue samples from the bodies of people who died of the 1918 Spanish Flu have proved that bird flu is a modern adaptation of that virus.

zh: 有记录的最早的流行病之一发生在大约公元500年至550年之间。
en: One of the earliest epidemics on record happened between about 500 and 550 CE.

zh: 科学家现在估计，该地区多达50%的人口死于这场流行病，使其成为罗马帝国灭亡的主要原因之一。
en: Scientists now estimate that up to 50% of the area's population died from the epidemic, making it one of the main causes of the fall of the Roman Empire.

zh: 随后，在14世纪30年代，这种流行病再次出现，这次是在亚洲。
en: Then, in the 1330s, this epidemic returned, this time in Asia.

zh: 它迅速传播到欧洲，并被称为“黑死病”，因为一个明显的症状是身体上出现黑斑。
en: It spread rapidly to Europe and became known as "The Black Death" because one visible symptom was black spots on the body.

zh: 几个世纪后，1918年的大流感，也称为西班牙流感，在第一次世界大战接近尾声时爆发。
en: Centuries later, the Great Flu Epidemic of 1918, also called the Spanish Flu, struck near the end of World War I.

zh: 那些刚刚熬过战争的家庭，现在又不得不埋葬死于这种疾病的亲人。
en: Families who had only just got through the war now had to bury their loved ones who had died of the disease.

zh: 由于战争，旅行和流动的人口可能比历史上任何时候都多，这导致了流行病在全球范围内传播。
en: As a result of the war, more people were travelling and moving perhaps than ever before in history and this caused the epidemic to spread worldwide.

zh: 西班牙流感在18个月内夺去了多达5000万人的生命，被认为是历史上最严重的流行病。
en: Having killed up to 50 million people in 18 months, the Spanish Flu is believed to have been the most severe epidemic in history.

zh: 死于流感的人比死于战争的人还要多。
en: More people died of the flu than died in the war.

zh: 起初，许多人认为这类流行病不再构成威胁，直到最近SARS成为令人担忧的原因。
en: Initially, many people believed that such epidemics no longer posed a threat until very recently when SARS became a cause for concern.

zh: SARS流行病始于2002年广东省的一例肺部疾病。
en: The SARS epidemic began in 2002 with a case of lung disease in Guangdong Province.

zh: 它传播到加拿大、越南和新加坡，并持续到2003年。
en: It spread to Canada, Vietnam and Singapore and lasted until 2003.

zh: 科学家们对SARS进行了研究，他们对这种新疾病感到困惑，并希望了解更多关于它及其病因的信息。
en: SARS was examined by scientists, who were confused by this new disease and wanted to learn more about it and its causes.

zh: 感染率稳步上升，在疫情爆发过程中，大约有8000人在九个月内被感染。
en: Infection rates went up steadily, and in the course of the outbreak, about 8,000 people were infected over nine months.

zh: 自SARS疫情结束以来，科学家们一直在进行治疗方法试验，以防止未来的任何爆发。
en: Since the end of the SARS epidemic, scientists have been doing trials on treatments to prevent any future outbreaks.

zh: 然后，从2013年到2016年，西非爆发了最广泛的埃博拉病毒病（EVD），导致一些西非国家遭受重大生命损失。
en: Then, from 2013 to 2016, there was the most widespread outbreak of the Ebola Virus Disease (EVD) in West Africa, which resulted in a major loss of life in a number of west African countries.

zh: 官方记录的死亡人数为11310人，但实际死亡人数可能要高得多。
en: 11,310 deaths were officially recorded, but the actual number of deaths is probably much higher.

zh: 该病毒传染性极强，在一些地区的存活率低至30%，表明很少有人能从中康复。
en: The virus was extremely infectious and the survival rate was as low as 30% in some areas, indicating that very few people got over it.

zh: 尽管（或者也许正是因为）世界近年来面临的流行病，世界各国政府都强调，他们希望对流行病进行彻底和系统的医学研究，以找出病因并尽快找到治疗方法。
en: Despite, or perhaps because of, the epidemics the world has faced in recent years, governments worldwide have underlined the fact that they want thorough and systematic medical research on epidemics to get at the causes and come up with cures as soon as possible.

zh: 各国需要共同努力做到这一点，因为全球各国之间的团队合作可以拯救生命。
en: Countries will need to work together to do this because teamwork among nations across the globe can save lives.

zh: 流行病是一种传染病在短时间内迅速传播，导致大量死亡，不同疾病的死亡人数各不相同。
en: An epidemic is the rapid spread of an infectious disease over a short period of time, causing a large number of deaths, the number of which differs for different diseases.

zh: 吉姆·史密斯教授进行了一项研究，将1997年发生的禽流感疫情与我们历史上的其他致命流行病进行了比较。
en: Professor Jim Smith conducted a study to compare the bird flu epidemic which occurred in 1997 with other deadly epidemics in our history.

zh: 他发现最早的流行病发生在公元500至550年，导致该特定地区一半的人口死亡。
en: He found that the earliest epidemics took place as early as 500 to 550 CE, which killed half of the population in that particular area.

zh: 同样的流行病，被称为“黑死病”，于1330年代在亚洲卷土重来，并迅速传播到欧洲。
en: The same epidemic, called "The Black Death" returned in the 1330s in Asia and spread quickly to Europe.

zh: 几百年后，1918年爆发了大流感，也称为西班牙流感，导致5000万人死亡。
en: Some hundred years later, the Great Flu epidemic broke out in 1918, also known as the Spanish Flu, killing 50 million people.

zh: 这被认为是人类历史上最严重的流行病。
en: This was considered the most severe epidemic in human history.

zh: 1997年夺走100多人生命的禽流感是1918年大流感的变种。
en: The 1997 bird flu which claimed over 100 lives was an adaptation of the Great Flu of 1918.

zh: 21世纪初有两种主要的流行病：SARS和EVD。
en: At the start of the 21st century there were two major epidemics: SARS and EVD.

zh: 较新发现的SARS疾病感染了约8000人。
en: The more recently discovered SARS disease infected about 8,000 people.

zh: 根据官方记录，仅在西非，EVD就已导致超过11,310人死亡。
en: According to official records EVD has killed more than 11,310 people in West Africa alone.

zh: 全球范围内，研究人员正共同努力，试图找出流行病的原因并帮助寻找有效的治疗方法。
en: Globally, researchers are coming together to try to identify the causes for and help to find effective treatments for epidemics.

zh: 中医（TCM）是一种医疗体系，起源于古代中国，并已发展了数千年。
en: Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) is a system of medical care which originated in ancient China and has evolved over thousands of years.

zh: 它发展出了一套独特的治疗方法组合，涉及促进健康以及疾病的预防和治疗。
en: It has developed a unique combination of treatments related to helping well-being and the prevention and treatment of diseases.

zh: 中医从业者认为身心没有分离。
en: Practitioners of TCM believe there is no separation between body and mind.

zh: 他们关注心、身和环境之间的相互作用。
en: They look at the interactions between mind, body and environment.

zh: 人体是更大的、周围宇宙的一个缩影。
en: The human body is a miniature version of the larger, surrounding universe.

zh: 两种对立而又互补的力量（称为阴和阳）之间的和谐支持健康，而疾病则源于这些力量之间的不平衡。
en: Harmony between two opposing yet complementary forces, called yin and yang, supports health, and disease results from an imbalance between these forces.

zh: 五行——金、木、水、火、土——象征性地代表了所有现象，包括人类生命的各个阶段，并解释了身体的功能以及它在生病或患病期间如何变化。
en: Five elements—metal, wood, water, fire and earth—symbolically represent all phenomena, including the stages of human life, and explain the functioning of the body and how it changes during an illness or disease.

zh: 气，一种流经身体的重要能量，在维持健康方面发挥着多种功能。
en: Qi, a vital energy that flows through the body, performs multiple functions in maintaining health.

zh: 中医治疗旨在通过恢复能量平衡来治愈问题。
en: TCM treatments aim to cure problems by restoring the balance of energies.

zh: 草药和针灸是最受欢迎的治疗方法。
en: Herbal medicine and acupuncture are the most popular treatments.

zh: 中药中使用的成分超过10,000种。
en: There are over 10,000 ingredients used in Chinese herbal medicine.

zh: 植物的不同部分及其提取物是最常用的成分，尽管一些动物和矿物成分也被使用。
en: Different parts of plants and their extracts are the most commonly used ingredients, although some animal and mineral ingredients are also used.

zh: 针灸是一种通过将极细的针插入皮肤来刺激身体特定部位的治疗方法。
en: Acupuncture is a treatment that stimulates specific points on the body by inserting extremely thin needles into the skin.

zh: 它通常用于缓解诸如腰痛、肩部僵硬和膝盖酸痛等病症的疼痛。
en: It is often used to provide pain relief for conditions like lower back pain, shoulder stiffness, and knee soreness.

zh: 其他疗法包括呼吸和运动练习、按摩疗法和饮食。
en: Other practices include breathing and movement exercises, massage therapy, and diet.

zh: 如今，DNA侦探可以帮助警察抓捕罪犯。
en: Nowadays, DNA detectives can help policemen catch criminals.

zh: 它已成为解决悬案的关键武器之一。
en: It has been one of the key weapons for unsolved cases.

zh: DNA被储存在数据库中，警察可以在其中找到相同或相似的DNA来追踪嫌疑人和受害者。
en: The DNA is stored in a database, where policemen can find the same or similar DNA to track down suspects and victims.

zh: 1984年6月8日，一名17岁的女孩梅兰妮·罗德在英国巴斯夜间独自回家的路上。
en: A 17-year-old girl, Melanie Road, was on her way home alone at night in Bath (UK) on 8 June, 1984.

zh: 第二天，她的尸体在一片血泊中被发现。
en: Her body was found the next day in a pool of blood.

zh: 警方讯问了几名嫌疑人，但无法确认杀害女孩的凶手。
en: Police questioned several suspects, but they couldn't identify the person who killed the girl.

zh: 从梅兰妮的尸体上，警方找到了凶手的完整DNA图谱。
en: From Melanie's body, the police have found a full DNA profile of the killer.

zh: 他们查阅了一个包含12000个当时是证人或嫌疑人的男性名字的数据库；但没有找到匹配项。
en: They worked through a database of 12,000 male names who were either witnesses or suspects at the time; but no match was found.

zh: 30年后，罪犯仍然没有被找到。
en: After 30 years, the criminal had still not been found.

zh: 梅兰妮的母亲给当地一家报纸写信：“我知道那是很久以前的事了——但对我来说就像昨天一样。
en: Melanie's mother wrote to a local newspaper: "I know it's a long time ago — to me it's only yesterday.

zh: 有些人说......让它过去吧，但我做不到。”
en: Some people say ... let it rest, but I can't."

zh: 2014年，一名妇女因一件小事被捕，她的DNA被录入了数据库。
en: In 2014, a woman was arrested for a minor incident and her DNA was profiled.

zh: 2015年7月，她的DNA与梅兰妮凶手的DNA之间找到了匹配。
en: In July 2015, a match was found between her DNA and the DNA of Melanie's killer.

zh: 警方发现这名妇女是凶手克里斯托弗·汉普顿的女儿。
en: The police found the woman was the daughter of the killer, Christopher Hampton.

zh: 31年后，罪犯终于被找到。
en: The criminal was finally found 31 years later.

zh: 哥白尼，这位近500年前去世的波兰天文学家，在21世纪通过DNA证据被确认了身份。
en: Copernicus, the Polish astronomer who died nearly 500 years ago, was identified by DNA evidence in the 21st century.

zh: 2005年，在波兰弗龙堡发现了一个头骨和不完整的骨架，哥白尼于1543年5月24日被埋葬于此。
en: In 2005, a skull and an incomplete skeleton were found in Frombork, Poland, where Copernicus was buried on 24 May, 1543.

zh: 这个头骨被用于身份鉴定，专家发现它属于一名70岁的男子，鼻子骨折，左眼上方有一道伤口。
en: The skull was used for identification and experts found that it had belonged to a 70-year-old man with a broken nose and a cut above his left eye.

zh: 哥白尼七十岁去世，因童年事故导致鼻子骨折，在一幅自画像中，他在左眼上方画了一道疤痕。
en: Copernicus died at seventy, had a broken nose as a result of a childhood accident and in a self-portrait painted himself with a scar above the left eye.

zh: 专家们能够重建这个人的脸，并且它与哥白尼的肖像相匹配。
en: Experts were able to reconstruct the man's face and it matched Copernicus' portraits.

zh: 然而，他们必须进行DNA测试来确认头骨主人的身份。
en: However, they had to perform DNA tests to confirm the identity of the skulls owner.

zh: 他们无法轻易找到哥白尼近亲的遗传物质，但他们能够找到他在乌普萨拉大学拥有几十年的一本书。
en: They couldn't easily find genetic material from Copernicus' close relatives, but they were able to find a book he had owned for decades at Uppsala University.

zh: 他们没花多长时间就在书页中找到了九根头发，其中一些带有良好的DNA样本。
en: They didn't have to look long to find nine hairs within the pages, some with good DNA samples.

zh: 头发中的DNA与在弗龙堡发现的骨头中的DNA相同。
en: The DNA from the hair was the same as that from the bones found at Frombork.

zh: 有趣的是，分析显示哥白尼可能有蓝眼睛，尽管他在肖像中通常是深色眼睛。
en: Interestingly, the analysis revealed that Copernicus probably had blue eyes, although he usually has dark eyes in his portraits.

zh: 一些科学家说，该团队不应该这么早宣布他们的发现——他们应该更仔细地寻找哥白尼母亲或她亲属的DNA来检查结果，因为是母亲的DNA完整地传给了她的孩子。
en: Some scientists say the team should not have announced their findings so early — they should have looked more carefully for the DNA from Copernicus' mother or her relatives to check the results, as it is the mother's DNA that is passed on intacts to her child.

zh: 尽管存在这些争议，这位伟大的天文学家于2010年5月22日在弗龙堡被正式重新安葬。
en: Despite these controversies, the great astronomer was officially buried again on 22 May, 2010 in Frombork.

zh: 对他母亲DNA的搜寻仍在继续。
en: The search for his mother's DNA still continues.

zh: 当一个卵子分裂并创造出同卵双胞胎时，就发生了克隆。
en: Cloning occurs when an egg splits and creates identical twins.

zh: 科学家们成功克隆的第一只动物是一只名叫多莉的羊。
en: The first animal to be successfully cloned by scientists was a sheep called Dolly.

zh: 尽管最初取得了成功，但她患上了一种疾病，并在六岁时死亡。
en: Despite the initial success, she suffered from an illness and died at the age of six.

zh: 从那时起，其他动物如山羊和骆驼也被克隆出来，但也经历了类似的健康问题。
en: Since then, other animals such as goats and camels have been cloned but have also experienced similar health problems.

zh: 科学家们不确定这个问题是否会永远持续发生。
en: Scientists are not sure if this problem will continue to happen forever.

zh: 与其他物种相比，克隆猴子的尝试要复杂得多，并花费了中国科学家许多年的时间。
en: In comparison with other species, attempts to clone monkeys have been far more complicated and took Chinese scientists many years.

zh: 他们想要突破这一技术障碍的原因是，猴子克隆可能为如何治愈疾病提供线索。
en: The reason why they wanted to break this technical barrier was that monkey cloning might offer clues as to how to cure illness and disease.

zh: 随着技术的改进和变得更加精确，将会有更大的好处。
en: As the technology improves and becomes more accurate there would be even greater benefits.

zh: 虽然有些人将克隆视为对医学和科学发展的福音，但其他人对其可能被使用的方式存在伦理上的担忧。
en: While some people see cloning as a blessing for medical and scientific development, others have ethical concerns about how it could be used.

zh: 他们担心这项技术将导致其他物种的克隆。
en: They worry that this technology will lead to cloning of other species.

zh: 存在这种技术可能被一些公司或个人滥用的风险。
en: There is a risk that this could be abused by some companies or people.

zh: 它对于人类是至关重要的福音还是诅咒，仍有待观察，并取决于我们选择如何使用它。
en: Whether it is a crucial blessing or a curse for humanity remains to be seen and depends on how we choose to use it.

zh: 大脑是人体中最重要的器官。
en: The brain is the most important organ in the human body.

zh: 它包含超过一千亿个神经细胞。
en: It contains over one hundred billion nerve cells.

zh: 我们大脑中的神经细胞通过电脉冲连接。
en: The nerve cells in our brain are connected by electrical impulses.

zh: 一个大脑中可能的连接比宇宙中的原子还要多。
en: There are more possible connections in one brain than there are atoms in the universe.

zh: 我们大脑的不同部分负责我们身体的不同部分。
en: Different parts of our brain are responsible for different parts of our body.

zh: 我怎样才能提高我的脑力？你有什么建议？
en: How can I increase my brain power? What's your advice?

zh: 我建议你做对心脏有益的运动。
en: I advise you to do exercise that is good for your heart.

zh: 这是个好主意。我应该改变我的饮食吗？
en: That's a good idea. Should I change my diet?

zh: 你可以做的一件事是减少吃垃圾食品。
en: One thing you could do is to cut down on junk food.

zh: 那我应该吃什么呢？
en: What should I eat then?

zh: 增加欧米伽-3的摄入量可能是个好主意。
en: It might be a good idea to increase your intake of Omega-3.

zh: 你可以尝试吃鱼、坚果和种子来增加欧米伽-3的摄入量。
en: You could try eating fish, nuts and seeds to increase your intake of Omega-3.

zh: 好的，我也在想我还能做些什么。
en: OK, I was also wondering what else I could do.

zh: 有没有什么特别的运动我可以做来帮助？你有什么建议？
en: Is there any particular kind of exercise I could do to help? What do you suggest?

zh: 你可以尝试学习一门新语言来保持大脑的灵活性。
en: You could try learning a new language to keep the brain flexible.

zh: 流行病是一种传染性疾病，在短时间内迅速传播给大量人群。
en: An epidemic is an infectious disease that spreads rapidly to a large number of people within a short period of time.

zh: 只要人类在地球上存在，流行病就一直在发生。
en: Epidemics have been occurring as long as humans have been on Earth.

zh: 一些流行病是先前病毒的变种。
en: Some epidemics are variations of previous viruses.

zh: 当某种疾病导致一定数量的死亡时，它就达到了流行病的水平，而这个数字对于每种疾病都是不同的。
en: A disease reaches epidemic levels when a certain number of deaths occur as a result of it and this number will be different for each disease.

zh: 一种疾病可能只导致少数死亡，但被归类为流行病，而与之相反的是其他疾病，如流感，它传播广泛但基线要高得多。
en: One disease might result in just a few deaths but be classified as an epidemic, as opposed to others such as the flu, which is widespread but has much higher baselines.

zh: 在人类历史上，曾发生过一些致命的流行病。
en: In human history, there have been some deadly epidemics.

zh: 在20世纪初，1918年的西班牙流感是一场大规模的流行病，估计导致5000万人死亡，比第一次世界大战中死亡的人数还要多。
en: In the early 20th century, the Spanish Flu of 1918 was a massive epidemic that killed an estimated 50 million people, more than the number of people who died in World War I.

zh: 最初，人们认为流行病对现代世界构成的威胁很小，直到2002年SARS爆发。
en: Initially, it was believed that epidemics posed little threat to the modern world, until SARS outbreak in 2002.

zh: SARS流行病最初在中国是一种肺部疾病，但最终蔓延到全球，传播到加拿大和越南等国家。
en: The SARS epidemic started as a lung disease in China but eventually spread across the globe to countries such as Canada and Vietnam.

zh: 它导致大约8000人感染，自那时起，科学家们进行了治疗试验，以防止未来的死亡。
en: It resulted in the infection of about 8,000 people and since then, scientists have undertaken trials on treatments to prevent future deaths.

zh: 玛丽·雪莱于1797年出生于伦敦。
en: Mary Shelley was born in 1797 in London.

zh: 1814年，玛丽遇见并爱上了诗人珀西·雪莱。
en: In 1814 Mary met and fell in love with Percy Shelley, the poet.

zh: 有一次，玛丽与珀西·雪莱以及另一位诗人拜伦勋爵一起游历欧洲。
en: Once Mary travelled around Europe with Percy Shelley and another poet, Lord Byron.

zh: 一天晚上，当他们住在日内瓦的一栋别墅里时，拜伦宣布：“我们每人写一个鬼故事。”
en: One night, while they were staying in a villa in Geneva, Byron announced, "We will each write a ghost story."

zh: 珀西·雪莱开始写一个故事但没有完成，拜伦写了一个关于吸血鬼的故事；而玛丽开始写《弗兰肯斯坦》。
en: Percy Shelley began a story but didn't finish it, Byron wrote a story about a vampire; and Mary began to write Frankenstein.

zh: 书中由怪物讲述的悲惨人生故事在电影制作中常常被忽略。
en: The sad life story told by the monster is often ignored in film productions of the book.

zh: 我的名字是沃尔顿船长。
en: My name is Captain Walton.

zh: 我的船正在北极探险时，我们发现了一个人站在一块浮冰上。
en: My ship was exploring the Arctic when we found a man on a piece of floating ice.

zh: 他叫维克多·弗兰肯斯坦。
en: He was called Victor Frankenstein.

zh: 一天晚上，他给我讲了一个奇怪的故事。
en: One night, he told me a strange tale.

zh: “我亲爱的船长，我该从何说起呢？
en: "My dear captain, how shall I begin?

zh: 我在日内瓦附近长大。
en: I grew up near Geneva.

zh: 我是一个快乐的孩子。
en: I was a happy child.

zh: 我的父母还有另外两个儿子，欧内斯特和威廉，他们还收养了一个孤儿，伊丽莎白。
en: My parents had two other sons, Ernest and William, and they took in an orphan, Elizabeth.

zh: 当我十七岁时，我去了因戈尔施塔特大学。
en: When I was seventeen, I went to the University of Ingolstadt.

zh: 那里的一位教授激励我学习化学。
en: A professor there inspired me to study chemistry.

zh: 这改变了我的生活，因为我变得痴迷于寻找生命的奥秘。
en: This changed my life, as I became obsessed with the search for the secret of life.

zh: 我日夜工作了两年，终于，我发现了这个秘密。
en: I worked day and night for two years and, finally, I found the secret.

zh: 为了检验我的发现，我决定在实验室里创造一个新的生命。
en: To test my discovery, I decided to create a new life in the laboratory.

zh: 我收集了身体的各个部分，两年后，我准备好让我的造物活过来。
en: I collected parts of bodies and, after two years, I was ready to bring my creature to life.

zh: 但当我看到我创造的怪物时，我感到恐惧和厌恶。
en: But when I saw the monster I had created, I felt horror and disgust.

zh: 我该如何描述这个怪物呢？
en: How can I describe the monster?

zh: 你可以看到他薄薄的、黄色的皮肤下的肌肉。
en: You could see the muscles under his thin, yellow skin.

zh: 他的头发又长又黑，牙齿洁白如珍珠，但他的眼睛水汪汪的，嘴唇是黑色的。
en: His hair was long and black, his teeth were pearly white, but his eyes were watery and his lips black.

zh: 同一天晚上，我的好朋友亨利·克莱瓦尔来了。
en: On the same night, my good friend Henry Clerval arrived.

zh: 见到他我很高兴，也松了一口气，我没有提及那个怪物，那时它已经离开了我的房子。
en: Happy and relieved to see him, I said nothing about the monster, which by that time had left my house.

zh: 亨利带来了我家人的消息。
en: Henry brought news of my family.

zh: 他们都很好，并且收留了一个叫贾斯汀的仆人。
en: They were well and had taken in a servant called Justine.

zh: 我所做之事的恐怖感随即向我袭来，我发起高烧，持续了几个月。
en: The horror of what I had done then hit me and I fell into a fever that lasted several months.

zh: 亨利照顾我直到康复，当我醒来时，他递给我一封在我生病期间寄到的信。
en: Henry nursed me back to health and when I awoke handed me a letter which had arrived during my illness.

zh: 想象一下我读信时的震惊——我的弟弟威廉被谋杀了！
en: Imagine my shock when I read it — my young brother William had been murdered!

zh: 我去了我父母的房子。
en: I went to my parents' house.

zh: 就在我到达之前，我看到那个怪物在树林中奔跑。
en: Just before I got there, I saw the monster running through the trees.

zh: 我意识到我创造的怪物杀死了我的弟弟！
en: I realised the monster I had created had killed my brother!

zh: 当我到达房子时，我惊恐地发现，在犯罪现场附近被发现的贾斯汀被指控为谋杀犯。
en: When I got to the house, I was horrified to discover that Justine, who had been found near the scene of the crime, was accused of the murder.

zh: 我知道她是无辜的，但却什么也不能说。
en: I knew she was innocent, but could say nothing.

zh: 不久之后，当我在山里独处时，我又遇到了我那邪恶的造物。
en: Soon afterwards, while I was spending some time alone in the mountains, I met my evil creation again.

zh: 怪物恳求我听他的故事。
en: The monster begged me to listen to his story.

zh: 他是这样说的：
en: This is what he said:

zh: 当我离开你家时，我遇到一间小屋，并秘密住在谷仓里观察一位失明的老人和他的孩子们。
en: When I left your house, I came across a cottage and lived secretly in the barn watching an old, blind man and his children.

zh: 我学会了说话，并意识到自己有多么悲惨。
en: I learned how to speak and I realised how miserable I was.

zh: 我没有家人，没有记忆，没有童年。
en: I had no family, no memories, no childhood.

zh: 有一天，我决定和那位老人说话。
en: One day, I decided to speak to the old man.

zh: 他很善良，而且看不见我丑陋的身体。
en: He was kind and couldn't see my ugly body.

zh: 但他的孩子们一回来，他们就尖叫并用棍子打我。
en: But as soon as his children returned, they screamed and hit me with sticks.

zh: 有一次，我从河里救了一个溺水的女孩，但她的朋友一看到我就向我开枪。
en: On one occasion, I saved a girl from drowning in a river, but when her friend saw me he fired a gun at me.

zh: 这就是善意的回报。
en: This was the reward of kindness.

zh: 我发誓对人类怀有永恒的仇恨和报复。
en: I promised eternal hatred and revenge on mankind.

zh: 我的第一个受害者是你年幼的弟弟。
en: My first victim was your young brother.

zh: 但现在我只想要一个伴侣。
en: But now I want nothing more than a companion.

zh: 我要你为我制造一个女性伴侣，我们将会一起远离所有人类生活，我保证。
en: I want you to make me a female companion and we will live together far away from all humans, I promise.

zh: 我同意了他的要求，尽管这个想法对我来说很可怕。
en: I agreed to his demand, though the idea was terrible to me.

zh: 当我回到家时，我父亲提到了他希望我娶伊丽莎白的愿望，我深爱着她。
en: When I returned home, my father mentioned his wish for me to marry Elizabeth, whom I loved dearly.

zh: 但在结婚之前，我需要去英格兰完成我的工作。
en: But before the marriage, I needed to go to England to complete my work.

zh: 亨利和我一起来到英格兰，最终，我完成了我的第二个创造物。
en: Henry came with me to England and, eventually, I completed my second creation.

zh: 当我赋予她生命时，我开始严重怀疑自己所做的一切。
en: As I was bringing her to life, I began to have serious doubts about what I was doing.

zh: 但是，突然，我的怪物出现了。
en: But, suddenly, my monster arrived.

zh: 他说：‘你是我的创造者，但我是你的主人！’
en: He said, 'You are my creator, but I am your master!'

zh: 在困惑中，我把她的身体撕成了碎片。
en: In my confusion, I broke her body into pieces.

zh: 怪物离开了，说道：‘我会在你新婚之夜陪着你！’
en: The monster left, saying, 'I will be with you on your wedding night!'

zh: 第二天警察逮捕了我。
en: The next day the police arrested me.

zh: 我被指控谋杀了一个年轻人。
en: I was accused of the murder of a young man.

zh: 他们给我看了尸体——那是我的朋友亨利！
en: They showed me the body — it was my friend Henry!

zh: 我的怪物已经夺走了他的第三个受害者。
en: My monster had claimed his third victim.

zh: 他们把我关进监狱；但最终，我被证明是无辜的。
en: They put me in prison; but, in the end, I was proved innocent.

zh: 我被怪物关于我新婚之夜所说的话所困扰，我想要摧毁他。
en: I was haunted by what the monster had said about my wedding night and I wanted to destroy him.

zh: 在我们的新婚之夜，我正在外面检查怪物是否出现，这时我听到了尖叫声。
en: On our wedding night, I was checking for the monster outside when I heard screams.

zh: 我跑上楼，却发现伊丽莎白死了！
en: I ran upstairs, only to find Elizabeth dead!

zh: 怪物就在窗边。
en: The monster was at the window.

zh: 他在跑进夜色中之前大笑起来。
en: He laughed before he ran off into the night.

zh: 摧毁这个怪物现在是我唯一的目标。
en: The destruction of the monster was now my only aim.

zh: 我追着他跑遍了整个地球——地中海、黑海、俄罗斯——但他总是逃脱。
en: I followed him all over the Earth — the Mediterranean, the Black Sea, Russia — but he always escaped.

zh: 我跟着他到了北极的冰冻荒原，但他总是领先我一步。
en: I followed him to the frozen wastes of the Arctic, but he was one step ahead.

zh: 亲爱的船长，当你发现我时，我正漂浮在一块碎冰上。
en: I was drifting on a piece of broken ice when, dear captain, you found me.

zh: 请相信我的故事，并向我保证你会尽你所能杀死这个怪物。
en: Please believe my tale and promise me you will do all you can to kill this monster.

zh: 那是他的遗言。
en: Those were his last words.

zh: 他在我的船舱里去世了。
en: He died in my cabin.

zh: 我走了出去，然后我听到了奇怪的声音。
en: I went out and then I heard strange noises.

zh: 当我回到船舱时，我看到那个怪物，一个可怕的生物，跪在弗兰肯斯坦旁边哭泣。
en: When I returned to my cabin I saw the monster, a horrible creature, kneeling next to Frankenstein and crying.

zh: 他告诉我他曾经多么悲惨，以及他感到多么内疚。
en: He told me how miserable he had been and how guilty he felt.

zh: 他想死，并向我保证他会自我毁灭。
en: He wanted to die and assured me he would destroy himself.

zh: 然后，他跳出船舱窗户跳到冰上，被海浪带入黑暗之中。
en: Then, he jumped out of the cabin window onto the ice and was taken away by the waves into the darkness.

zh: 如果你最近在学校智商测试中表现出色，你可能会自豪地宣布你的结果，并相信这个结果预示着你未来的人生成功。
en: If you have recently done well in an IQ test in school you may have proudly announced your results and believed that the result predicted your future success in life.

zh: 大多数人倾向于将智商测试的好成绩与智力联系起来，但信不信由你，这个问题比你最初意识到的要复杂得多。
en: Most people tend to associate good results in IQ tests with intelligence but believe it or not, there is more to the issue than you may first realise.

zh: 作为一个人力资源经理，我一直在面试人。
en: As a human resources manager, I interview people all the time.

zh: 如果你的唯一目标是受雇，那么一个好的智商可能会对此有所帮助。
en: If your only aim is to be employed, well then a good IQ would probably help that.

zh: 然而，如果你真的想在你的职业生涯中取得成功，你还需要高情商或EQ。
en: However, if you really want to succeed in your career you also need a high Emotional Quotient or EQ.

zh: 高情商分数的人对压力和紧张反应更好，并且能适应不同的环境和情况。
en: People with high EQ scores react better to stress and pressure and can adjust to different environments and situations.

zh: 一些高智商的人也有高情商，但并不总是如此。
en: Some people with high IQs also have high EQs but not always.

zh: 其他人可能有高情商但智商较低。
en: Others may have high EQs but a lower IQ.

zh: 我的建议是记住要同时专注于发展你的情感和学术技能，你会看到好处的。
