en: My advice is to remember to focus on developing both your emotional and academic skills altogether and you will see the benefits.

zh: 有许多新技能对未来就业很有用。
en: There are many new skills that will be useful for employment in the future.

zh: 在未来的岁月里，当然还会有传统的工作，如医生、牙医和律师，但也有许多新的工作领域。
en: In years to come there will of course, still be traditional jobs such as doctors, dentists and lawyers but also lots of new areas to work in.

zh: 例如，生物技术产业一直在增长。
en: For example, the bio-technology industry is growing all the time.

zh: 许多传统工作，如工厂工作和行政工作，将由人工智能完成，因此发展新技能对工人来说将很重要。
en: Lots of traditional jobs such as factory work and administrative jobs will be done by AI, so developing new skills will be important for workers.

zh: 最重要的技能是理解和批判性思维，这两者都有助于你在工作中变得更逻辑，并说服他人同意你的方法。
en: The most important skills are comprehension and critical thinking, both of which help you to become more logical in your work and persuade others to agree with your approaches.

zh: 管理金钱和预算的能力总是有用的，所以也要努力发展良好的财务技能。
en: The ability to manage money and budgets is always useful, so work on developing good financial skills too.

zh: 当然，不可能确切知道未来会发生什么。
en: Of course, it's impossible to know exactly what will happen in the future.

zh: 我们可以观察趋势，但没有保证。
en: We can look at trends but there are no guarantees.

zh: 最好的做法是始终保持学习并保持积极性。
en: The best thing to do is to always keep learning and stay motivated.

zh: 昨天我听了一个关于未来职业的非常有趣的播客。
en: I listened to a really interesting podcast about future careers yesterday.

zh: 它说我们在一生中预计会有很多份工作，而不仅仅是一份。
en: It said that we can expect to have many jobs over the course of our lives rather than just one.

zh: 如今，就业市场的变化速度非常快，并且这种情况在未来将持续下去。
en: Things are changing at a very fast speed these days in the job market and this will continue in the future.

zh: 虽然总会有机会获得令人满意的收入，但如果你想要真正取得伟大的成就，你应该立志成为某一特定领域的高技能人才。
en: Although there will always be the opportunity to earn a satisfactory income, if you want to really achieve great things, you should aim to become a highly skilled person in one particular area.

zh: 人们很有可能定期重新培训以学习更多技能，直到他们达到停止工作的时候。
en: There is a strong chance that people will regularly train again to learn more skills until they reach the time they stop working.

zh: 播客还谈到了未来绝对必要的技能类型，例如批判性思维。
en: The podcast also talked about the types of skills that will be absolutely necessary in the future such as critical thinking.

zh: 我迫不及待地想看看未来会带来什么！
en: I can't wait to see what the future brings!

zh: 21世纪的员工面临着他们父母一代从未经历过的挑战。
en: Employees in the 21st century are faced with challenges that their parents' generation never experienced.

zh: 为他们设定的标准比他们父母的标准更高，并且他们被期望拥有更多的资格和更广泛的技能。
en: Standards are set higher for them than they were for their parents and they are expected to have more qualifications and a wider range of skills.

zh: 21世纪员工将为之工作的公司类型也有所不同。
en: The type of companies 21st century employees will work for is also different.

zh: 他们中越来越多的人将在初创公司工作，这些公司被认为提供了比他们父母过去习惯的更动态和更令人兴奋的工作环境。
en: An increasing number of them will work at start-ups, which are seen as providing a more dynamic and exciting working environment than their parents would have been used to.

zh: 被投入一个高度互联的工作场所，今天的毕业生还必须准备好跳槽到不同的公司。
en: Thrown into a hyper-connected workplace, today's graduates also have to be prepared to move around to different companies.

zh: 或者，你可以决定完全离开传统的职业道路，创办自己的公司，这意味着所有的决定都可以由你而不是你的老板或你的公司来做。
en: Alternatively, you can decide to leave the traditional career path altogether and set up your own company, which means that all the decisions can be made by you instead of your boss or your company.

zh: 你知道我们下个月要去动物园进行学校旅行吗？
en: Did you know we're going on a school trip to the zoo next month?

zh: 你对动物园有什么看法？
en: What do you think about zoos?

zh: 在我看来，它们是一件好事。
en: In my opinion, they are a good thing.

zh: 嗯，我不确定我同意这一点。
en: Well, I'm not sure that I agree with that.

zh: 我不喜欢看到动物被关在笼子里。
en: I don't like to see animals in cages.

zh: 笼子并不总是对动物有害。
en: Cages aren't always bad for the animals.

zh: 野生动物可能很危险，所以我们必须把它们关起来。
en: Wild animals can be dangerous, so we have to keep them enclosed.

zh: 很多人曾被野生动物伤害过。
en: A lot of people have been injured by animals in the wild.

zh: 至少在动物园里它们是被控制住的。
en: At least they're kept under control in a zoo.

zh: 米里亚姆：但我知道动物园的动物有时也会攻击人。
en: Miriam: but I know zoo animals have sometimes attacked people too.

zh: 爱德华：也许我们应该少担心动物园动物可能带来的问题，只是享受在动物园度过一个美好、轻松的一天。
en: Edward: Perhaps we should worry less about the possible problems with zoo animals and just enjoy having a nice, relaxing day out at the zoo.

zh: 从现在到21世纪，世界上最富有和最先进国家的公民将发现越来越难以跟上变革的需求。
en: Between now and the 21st century, citizens of the world's richest and most advanced nations will find it more and more difficult to keep up with the demand for change.

zh: 对他们来说，未来将过早地到来。
en: For them, the future will arrive too soon.

zh: 现在我们发现自己生活在托夫勒设想的时间线的尽头，我们仍然经历着快速的变化，尤其是在工作领域。
en: Now we find ourselves living at the end of the timeline that Toffler imagined and we're still experiencing rapid changes, not least in the world of work.

zh: 某些领域的工作，如农业、煤炭开采和重工业，近年来已经减少，并且这种趋势很可能在未来几十年继续下去。
en: Jobs in certain areas, such as farming, coal mining and heavy industry, have declined in recent years and this trend is very likely to continue in the decades to come.

zh: 另一方面，科技领域的工作数量将继续显著增加。
en: On the other hand, the number of jobs in the tech sector will continue to increase significantly.

zh: 它目前雇佣了大约20%的劳动力。
en: It currently employs around 20% of the workforce.

zh: 当今的年轻人还需要意识到并为之准备的职场中还有许多其他重要变化。
en: There are also many other important changes happening in the world of work that young people today need to be aware of and prepare for.

zh: 首先，现在拥有“终身职业”的人越来越少。
en: First, fewer and fewer people now have a "job for life".

zh: 根据研究人员的说法，未来将有相当多的人会围绕多种工作来组织他们的工作生活，而不是像工程师、教师或会计师那样度过一生。
en: According to researchers, considerably more people will organise their working lives around a variety of jobs in the future, instead of spending their whole lives being an engineer, a teacher or an accountant, for example.

zh: 人们还需要更多地流动，以便他们可以在不同的公司从事不同的工作，因为随着内部晋升机会的减少，这将为他们提供攀登职业阶梯的最佳机会。
en: People will also need to move around more so they can take different jobs at different companies, because this will give them their best chance of climbing the career ladder, as the number of internal promotions falls.

zh: 也会有更多的人为小型、充满活力的公司工作，例如初创公司，它们能够快速响应市场变化。
en: Many more people will work for small, dynamic companies too, such as start-ups, which can quickly respond to changes in the market.

zh: 越来越多的人将完全不再作为公司雇员工作，而是创办自己的公司。
en: Increasing numbers of people will stop working as company employees altogether and start their own companies instead.

zh: 夜晚又冷又黑。
en: The evening was cold and dark.

zh: 他换下衣服，溜上了床。
en: He changed out of his clothing and slipped into bed.

zh: 即使盖着厚厚的毯子，他仍然感到不舒服的寒冷。
en: He still felt uncomfortably cold, even under a heavy blanket.

zh: 这房子很旧，有三层楼，看起来像是一个多年无人打理的地方。
en: The house was old with three-story and a look of somewhere that had not been taken care of for many years.

zh: 卧室的窗帘在吹过旧窗框的风中摇摆。
en: The curtain in the bedroom swayed in the wind that blew through the old window frame.

zh: 如果有人曾告诉他，在这么久之后他会回到这里，他会用怀疑的目光看着他们。
en: If someone had ever told him that he would be back here after so long, he would have looked at them with disbelief.

zh: 他陷入了不安的睡眠。
en: He fell into a restless sleep.

zh: 他梦见自己在一艘船上，航行在黑色、暴风雨的海上。
en: He dreamt he was on a boat, sailing through a black, stormy sea.

zh: 当他看着海浪拍打时，他感觉自己向后倒去，并大叫着醒来。
en: As he looked at the waves crashing, he felt himself falling backwards and awoke with a shout.

zh: 他睁开眼睛，环顾四周。
en: He opened his eyes and looked around.

zh: 他能感觉到房间里有一个看不见的人，并确信他看到门口有一双黑色的眼睛正盯着他。
en: He could sense some unseen person in the room and was certain that he saw a pair of dark eyes near the door staring at him.

zh: 他的心跳开始加快，他感到一种可怕的不安。
en: His heart began to beat faster and he felt a terrible unease.

zh: 我给任何希望成为作家或诗人的人的建议是，尽可能多地倾听你的情感和感受。
en: My advice to anyone hoping to become a writer or a poet, is to listen to your emotions and feelings as much as possible.

zh: 也非常推荐走出去，到大自然中去。
en: Getting out and about in nature is also highly recommended.

zh: 你永远不知道，在结冰的湖边或海湾附近散步会让人想起什么样的感受和词语。
en: You never know what kind of feelings and words, a walk near a frozen lake or a bay can bring to mind.

zh: 大多数人认为诗歌必须总是押韵，但事实并非如此。
en: Most people think that poetry must always rhyme but that's not the case.

zh: 然而，许多诗歌确实有一种连续的词语流，对读者来说是流畅和愉快的。
en: However, many poems do have a continuous flow of words which are smooth and enjoyable for the reader.

zh: 另一方面，一些诗歌的结构是为了强调某些词而不是其他词。
en: On the other hand, some poems are structured so that there is emphasis on some words and not others.

zh: 我推荐阅读一些来自历史上不同时代著名诗人的类似诗歌作品集。
en: I recommend reading some collections of similar works of poetry from famous poets from different eras in history.

zh: 看到人们对生活和爱的感受如此相似，尽管他们之间相隔数百年，真是令人惊叹。
en: It's amazing to see how similar people have felt about life and love, despite hundreds of years between them.

zh: 对于作家来说，也有一些关键技能需要学习，比如如何发展有趣的角色以及决定一种语调。
en: For writers, there are also key skills to learn, such as how to develop interesting characters and deciding on a tone.

zh: 如果你在写戏剧性的作品，重要的是让观众对接下来可能发生的事情保持兴趣和兴奋。
en: If you're writing dramatic pieces, it's important to keep the audience interested and excited about what might happen next.

zh: 这些人在海上待了太久，以至于很难回忆起陆地的模样。
en: The men had been at sea for so long that it was hard to remember what land looked like.

zh: 兴奋情绪在他们中间逐渐积累，因为麦考密克船长曾说他们距离圣马丁岛只有三十多里格，那里有阳光、干燥的陆地和新鮮的食物在等待着他们。
en: Excitement had been building among them as Captain McCormick had said that they were a little more than 30 leagues from the island of St Martins where sunshine, dry land and fresh food awaited them.

zh: 至少这是大卫军官说他偷听到的，当船长与巴纳比指挥官谈话时。
en: At least that's what Officer David said he had overheard when the Captain spoke to Commander Barnaby.

zh: 这个消息来得正是时候。
en: This news came at the right time.

zh: 在过去的几周里，一种日益增长的绝望感在这些人中间蔓延。
en: In the past few weeks, an increasing sense of desperation had been growing among the men.

zh: 它始于暴风雨之夜。
en: It began the night of the storm.

zh: 猛烈、狂暴的波浪几乎摧毁了船只。
en: Crashing, violent waves had nearly destroyed the ship.

zh: 他们以为他们会沉没，但庆幸的是，坚固的木制框架和帆都保持完好。
en: They thought they would sink but thankfully, the solid wooden frame and sails had held together.

zh: 不幸的是，他们丢失了一些食物，这意味着他们不能再一天吃三顿饭了。
en: Unfortunately, they had lost some food which meant they could no longer have three meals a day.

zh: 尽管如此，他们活了下来，这才是最重要的。
en: Nevertheless, they had survived and that is what mattered.

zh: 当黎明来临，太阳的第一缕光芒照亮了天空。
en: The first light of the sun lit up the sky as daybreak was approaching.

zh: 渐逝的月光在平静的海面上留下了一种闪亮、光滑的外观。
en: The dying moonlight left a shiny, polished look on the calm sea.

zh: 很难相信就是这同一片海，几乎夺走了他们的生命。
en: It was hard to believe the same sea, which nearly claimed their lives.

zh: 英语中最常被问到的问题之一是“你好吗？”，无论一个人感到快乐或悲伤、兴奋或孤独、奇妙或心碎，他们几乎总是回答“很好”。
en: One of the most commonly asked questions in the English language is "How are you?", and whether a person is feeling happy or sad, excited or lonely, fantastic or heartbroken, they will just about always answer "Fine".

zh: 这是为什么？是因为人类不擅长表达情感吗？也许只是因为情感往往太个人化或太复杂，无法在随意交谈中分享。
en: Why is this? Is it because humans are not good at expressing their emotions? Maybe it is just that emotions are often too personal or too complex to be shared in a casual conversation.

zh: 然而，几个世纪以来，人们经常以诗歌的形式在纸上表达情感。
en: However, for centuries, people have often expressed their emotions on paper, in the form of poetry.

zh: 诗歌的美在于诗人试图传达的情感常常隐藏在意象中，这样诗人可以保持一种隐私感。
en: The beauty of poetry is that the emotions the poet is trying to convey are often disguised in images so that the poet can maintain a feeling of privacy.

zh: 例如，在李白的诗《黄鹤楼送孟浩然之广陵》中，诗人描述了他朋友船的帆消失在远方，而他看着江水滚滚流过。
en: For example, in Li Bai's poem, "Seeing Meng Haoran Off at Yellow Crane Tower", the poet describes the sail of his friend's boat disappearing into the distance while he watches the river rolling by.

zh: 这可能看起来仅仅是对美丽景色的描述，但通过深入阅读这首诗，你可以感受到朋友离开给他带来的深深悲伤和孤独。
en: This could seem merely like the description of a beautiful scene, but by reading into the poem, you can sense the deep sadness and loneliness that his friend's leaving has caused him.

zh: 诗歌也被用来庆祝积极的人类情感，如喜悦、爱。
en: Poetry is also written to celebrate positive human emotions like joy, love.

zh: 纵观历史，男人和女人都曾为所爱之人写诗，以表达他们深厚的感情。
en: Throughout history, men and women have written poems to their loved ones to express their deep feelings.

zh: 关于自然的诗歌也很常见，它们表达了诗人对其周围世界之美的欣赏。
en: Poems about nature, which express the poet's appreciation of the beauty in the world around him or her, are also common.

zh: 可悲的是，也有许多诗歌被写出来表达对自然破坏的愤怒，以及对战争和死亡等恐怖事件的反应。
en: Sadly, many poems have also been written to express anger at the destruction of nature and reactions to horrific events like war and death.

zh: 诗歌的有趣之处在于，任何读诗的人都可以对其有自己的看法或感受，并且经常会对诗人最初想说什么或表达什么产生热烈的讨论。
en: The interesting thing about poetry is that anyone reading a poem can have their own view or feelings about it and heated discussions can often arise over what the poet was originally trying to say or express.

zh: 诗歌可以用许多不同的方式来诠释。
en: Poetry can be interpreted in many different ways.

zh: 通常，一个人喜欢的诗另一个人却讨厌。
en: Often, a poem that one person loves another hates.

zh: 同样地，一个人理解并产生共鸣的诗，可能会让另一个人感到厌烦。
en: Equally, a poem that one person understands and relates to, may turn another person off.

zh: 本："韩茜，你看过电影《侏罗纪公园》吗？你认为在这么多年后克隆一个物种是可能的吗？"
en: Ben: "Han Xi, have you seen the movie Jurassic Park? Do you think it is possible to clone a species after so many years?"

zh: 韩茜："嗯，电影让它看起来如此真实，并且让克隆灭绝动物的想法听起来非常令人兴奋，但电影在科学方面并不是很准确。"
en: Han Xi: "Hmmm, the movie makes it look so real, and makes the idea of cloning extinct animals sound very exciting, but movies are not very accurate when it comes to science."

zh: 本："这当然必定会让人思考这些可能性。"
en: Ben: "It certainly is bound to be make one think about the possibilities."

zh: 本："我的意思是——与绵羊相比，这要令人兴奋得多。"
en: Ben: "I mean - compared to sheep, it's a lot more exciting."

zh: 韩茜："我知道！但正确地做到这一点至关重要，否则它们可能会变得像电影里的那些一样太聪明和凶猛。"
en: Han Xi: "I know! But it would be crucial to get it right, or they could become too smart and fierce like the ones in the movie."

zh: 韩茜："在现实生活中看到如此神奇的生物将是一种莫大的幸事，但我知道这仅仅是一个幻想。"
en: Han Xi: "It would be such a blessing to see such amazing creatures in real life, but I know it's merely a fantasy."

zh: 大多数人都意识到保持身体健康的需要，但他们的头脑呢？
en: Most people are aware of the need to keep their bodies healthy but what about their minds?

zh: 你的大脑是身体的一个器官，就像你的心脏一样，所以它需要锻炼！
en: Your brain is an organ of the body, just like your heart, so it needs to be exercised!

zh: 我去年退休了，我发现仅仅几个月后，我就开始健忘，经常找不到像我的钥匙这样的物品。
en: I retired last year and I found that after merely a few months, I was getting forgetful and often couldn't locate items such as my keys.

zh: 我意识到我需要对我的生活方式做一些改变。
en: I realised that I needed to make some changes to my lifestyle.

zh: 现在我做报纸上的填字游戏和我在网上找到的其他文字游戏。
en: Now I do crosswords in the newspaper and other word puzzles I find online.

zh: 它帮助我保持警觉，我的头脑感觉清晰。
en: It helps keep me alert and my mind feels clear.

zh: 我还意识到我的饮食真的很差，充满了垃圾食品。
en: I also realised that my diet was really poor and full of junk food.

zh: 现在我已经增加了水果和蔬菜的摄入量。
en: Now I've increased my intake of fruit and vegetables.

zh: 它们含有许多对我的大脑非常有益的维生素和矿物质。
en: They contain lots of vitamins and minerals that are so good for my brain.

zh: 最后，可能对我来说最困难的是加入一些新的俱乐部。
en: Finally, and probably the most difficult for me was joining some new clubs.

zh: 在我这个年纪，走出舒适区很重要。
en: It's important at my age to get out of my comfort zone.

zh: 结交新朋友和学习新技能可以保持大脑活跃。
en: Making new friends and learning new skills keep the brain active.

zh: 我在我当地加入了一个国际象棋俱乐部，我非常喜欢它。
en: I joined a chess club in my local area and I enjoy it so much.

zh: 结核病（TB）是一种严重的传染性疾病，可以轻易地从一个人传播给另一个人。
en: Tuberculosis (TB) is a serious infectious disease that can easily spread from one person to another.

zh: 它影响肺部，并通过咳嗽或打喷嚏时释放的微小唾液滴传播。
en: It affects the lungs and is passed on through tiny drops of saliva released when someone coughs or sneezes.

zh: 由于对这种疾病及其治疗方法的更好理解，它在发达国家现在相当罕见。
en: Due to better understanding of the disease and how to treat it, it is now quite rare in the developed world.

zh: 全球部分地区，如非洲、亚洲和拉丁美洲的一些地区，结核病的发病率较高。
en: Parts of the globe, such as some parts of Africa, Asia and Latin America experience higher rates of TB.

zh: 然而，在人类免疫缺陷病毒（HIV）高发的国家，结核病的发病率也相当高，因为结核病在免疫系统较弱的人群中传播迅速。
en: In nations with high rates of human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), however, the incidence of TB is also quite high, as TB spreads quickly among people with weak immune systems.

zh: 感染该疾病的其他风险因素包括年龄（非常年轻或年老）、饥饿和营养不良，以及患有严重疾病如癌症或糖尿病。
en: Other risky factors for catching the disease include age (being very young or old), hunger and malnutrition and having serious illnesses such as cancer or diabetes.

zh: 生活在贫困或不健康的环境中，或在医院和其他卫生设施工作，也会给人们带来风险。
en: Living in poor or unhealthy conditions or working in hospitals and other health facilities also pose a risk to people.

zh: 有一些疫苗可以用于幼儿预防结核病，但它们对成人无效。
en: There are some vaccines that can be used in young children to prevent TB but they are not effective for adults.

zh: 其他疫苗的试验和测试正在进行中。
en: Trials and testing of other vaccines are ongoing.

zh: 你能给我什么建议，让我变得更健康？
en: What tips would you give me so I can get fitter?

zh: 嗯，你通常一周做多少运动？
en: Well, how much exercise do you do in a typical week?

zh: 我目前不做任何运动。
en: I don't do any exercise at the moment.

zh: 那你绝对应该开始做一些运动，否则你永远不会变得健康。
en: You should definitely start doing some exercise then, otherwise you'll never get fit.

zh: 告诉我你在空闲时间喜欢做什么。
en: Tell me what you like doing in your free time.

zh: 根据科学家的说法，第一个能活到150岁的人可能已经出生了。
en: The first person to live to 150 may already have been born, according to scientists.

zh: 在过去的200年里，全球预期寿命已经翻了一倍多，而最近的研究表明它尚未达到顶峰。
en: Worldwide life expectancy has more than doubled in the last 200 years and recent research shows it yet to reach a peak.

zh: 那么，活到能见到你的玄孙及更远的后代，生活会是什么样子？
en: So, what will life look like living to see your great-great-grandchildren and beyond?

zh: 寿命的延长是否意味着多年的疾病和虚弱？
en: Would increased longevity mean years of sickness and frailty?

zh: 长寿是现代世界的伟大成就之一，科学开始发现证据表明我们的基因与是否能活得更久有很大关系。
en: Longevity is one of the modern world's great successes and science is beginning to find evidence that our genes have a lot to do with whether we will live a longer life.

zh: 美国哈佛医学院的研究人员确信他们发现了一种所谓的'长寿基因'，并正在测试将这种基因添加到小鼠身上是否能延长它们的寿命。
en: Researchers at Harvard Medical School in the US are convinced they have found a so called 'longevity gene' and are testing whether the addition of this gene to mice extends their lives.

zh: 长期目标是找到一种通过基因疗法操控该基因的方法，为人类寿命增加一二十年。
en: The long-term aim is to find a way of manipulating the gene to add an extra decade or two to human life with gene therapy.

zh: 但是，你现在还能采取哪些其他因素来延长寿命？
en: But, what other factors can you adopt now to extend your life?

zh: 每周只需三次30分钟的温和运动就能增加我们的预期寿命。
en: Just 30 minutes of gentle exercise three times a week can add years to our life expectancy.

zh: 其次，少量你喜欢的东西对你有好处！
en: Secondly, a little of what you like does you good!

zh: 科学家认为，少量摄入我们通常归类为有害的东西可能对我们有益，例如巧克力、咖啡或日光浴！
en: Scientists believe that having small amounts of things we would normally classify as harmful might be good for us, such as chocolate, coffee or sunbathing!

zh: 第三，你居住的地方会影响你的生活。
en: Thirdly, where you live can influence your life.

zh: 所以尽量住在宁静祥和、与邻居相处融洽的地方。
en: So try to live somewhere peaceful and quiet where you get on with your neighbours.

zh: 其他因素包括食用富含水果、蔬菜、纤维和Omega-3油的健康饮食，并保持活跃的思维。
en: Other factors include eating a healthy diet high in fruit, vegetables, fibre and Omega-3 oils, and keeping an active mind.

zh: 社区感也有帮助，与家人和朋友保持联系。
en: A sense of community can help too, keeping in touch with family and friends.

zh: 因此，得益于未来的科学进步，活到150岁对今天的青少年来说可能是一个现实的期望。
en: So, living to 150 might be a realistic expectation for teenagers today thanks to future scientific advances.

zh: 你与生俱来的基因有一定程度的命运，但你也可以通过确保自己保持更长时间的健身和健康来帮助自己。
en: There is a certain amount of destiny with the genes you are born with but also you can help yourself by ensuring you stay fit and healthy for longer.

zh: 表示 “不太可能发生......”。
en: unlikely to + bare infinitive: to express the idea that something is not expected to happen

zh: 罗比不太可能得到这份工作。
en: Robbie is unlikely to get the job.

zh: 他没有必要的经验。
en: He doesn't have the necessary experience.

zh: 他们不太可能失业。
en: They are unlikely to lose their jobs.

zh: 他们在公司扮演着重要的角色。
en: They play an important role in the company.

zh: 玛丽亚通过了考试，这几乎不令人惊讶。
en: It is hardly a surprise that Maria passed her exams.

zh: 玛丽亚对自己通过考试几乎不感到惊讶。
en: Maria was hardly surprised that she passed her exams.

zh: 玛丽亚通过了考试，这几乎不令人惊讶。
en: It is hardly surprising that Maria passed her exams.

zh: 英语中动词可以分为两类：状态动词和动作动词。
en: In English, verbs can be divided into two groups: state verbs and activity verbs.

zh: 动作动词描述动作，可用于一般时态和进行时态。
en: Activity verbs describe activities and can be used in simple and continuous tenses.

zh: 状态动词描述状态，一般不用于进行时态。
en: State verbs describe states and are not usually used in continuous tenses.

zh: 以下是最常见的状态动词：
en: Here are the most common state verbs:

zh: 和表达思维活动的动词
en: Verbs related to activities of the mind

zh: admit, agree, believe; know, mean, prefer, realise, remember, think, understand, want...
en: admit, agree, believe, know, mean, prefer, realise, remember, think, understand, want...

zh: adore, care, like, dislike, love, hate, hope...
en: adore, care, like, dislike, love, hate, hope...

zh: 表示拥有和存在的动词
en: Verbs that describe having and being

zh: appear, be, belong, contain, have, include, need, seem, possess, own...
en: appear, be, belong, contain, have, include, need, seem, possess, own...

zh: feel, hear, look, see, smell, sound, taste ...
en: feel, hear, look, see, smell, sound, taste ...

zh: 有些状态动词也可以表示动作。
en: Some state verbs can also refer to an activity.

zh: 在这种语境下，这些动词可以用于进行时态。
en: In this context they can be used in the continuous tense.

zh: 我们有一辆二手车。
en: We have a second-hand car.

zh: (have = 拥有，一种状态)
en: (have = possess, a state)

zh: 她正在喝茶。
en: She is having some tea.

zh: (have = 喝，一个动作)
en: (have = drink, an activity)

zh: 我认为这是个好主意。
en: I think it's a great idea.

zh: (think = 认为，一种状态)
en: (think = believe, a state)

zh: 我正在考虑我的考试。
en: I'm thinking about my exam.

zh: (think = 考虑，一个动作)
en: (think = consider, an activity)

zh: 我觉得我无法完成它。
en: I feel I can't go through with it.

zh: (feel = 觉得/认为，一种状态)
en: (feel = believe, a state)

zh: (feel = 感受/体验，一个动作)
en: (feel = experience, an activity)

zh: 感官动词后接形容词，而不是副词：
en: Verbs related to senses are followed by adjectives, not adverbs:

zh: 这些玫瑰看起来和闻起来都很美。
en: The roses look and smell beautiful.

zh: 完成时表示一个完成的动作。
en: We use the perfect to express a completed action.

zh: 用来表示现在、过去或者未来某个时间点已经或者将会完成的动作。
en: It is used in the present, past and future tenses to refer to something that has, had or will be done by a certain time.

zh: 完成时的构成一般是动词have+ verb-ed形式。
en: It is formed using the verb "have" and verb-ed forms.

zh: 我喜欢这份工作。
en: I have enjoyed working in this job.

zh: 她已经考虑这件事很长时间了。
en: She has thought about it for a long time.

zh: 他们都同意这是最佳的行动方案。
en: They have all agreed that it is the best course of action.

zh: 园丁们从植物上取下插条并将其放入合适的培养基中时，一直在使用一种克隆形式。
en: Gardeners have been using a form of cloning when they take a cutting from a plant and place it in a suitable medium.

zh: 她已经等了一个小时。
en: She has been waiting for an hour.

zh: 我们小时候去过那个国家。
en: We had visited the country when we were children.

zh: 我在那个俱乐部打网球已经超过十年了。
en: I had played tennis in that club for over a decade.

zh: 我打电话时她已经去电影院了。
en: She had gone to the cinema when I called.

zh: 他说他们在过去两年里一直在对山羊进行实验。
en: He said they had been doing experiments on goats for the past two years.

zh: 戴夫已经开了一个小时的车，这时他的车坏了。
en: Dave had been driving for an hour when his car broke down.

zh: 短语动词是由两三个单词组成的动词。
en: Phrasal verbs are verbs that are made up of two or three words.

zh: 可以是及物的（带宾语）也可以是不及物的（不带宾语）。
en: They can be either transitive (they have an object) or intransitive (they have no object).

zh: 短语动词比较复杂，很多短语形式相似意思却不同。
en: Phrasal verbs can be complex as many can look similar but have very different meanings, e.g. take on, take over, take down.

zh: 她让我们把所有海报都取下来。
en: She made us take down all the posters.

zh: 有的短语动词中间可以插入名词，有的则不能。
en: Some phrasal verbs can be separated by the noun whereas others cannot.

zh: 可插入名词的，如:
en: Examples of separable phrasal verbs:

zh: 我离开办公室时忘了关电脑。
en: I forgot to turn off my computer when I left the office.

zh: 我离开办公室时忘了关电脑。
en: I forgot to turn my computer off when I left the office.

zh: 他意外地扔掉了他的笔记本。
en: He threw away his notebook, accidentally.

zh: 他意外地扔掉了他的笔记本。
en: He threw his notebook away, accidentally.

zh: 不可拆分的短语动词，如:
en: Examples of inseparable phrasal verbs:

zh: 我在报纸上偶然发现了一篇有趣的文章。
en: I came across an interesting article in the newspaper.

zh: 我期待着和我的妹妹共度时光。
en: I'm looking forward to spending time with my sister.

zh: 学习短语动词的困难在于，没有规则确定短语动词是及物或不及物,可拆分或不可拆分。
en: Unfortunately, there are no rules to help understand whether a phrasal verb is transitive / intransitive or separable / inseparable.

zh: 我对公司在市场经济下如何发展以及如何说服客户购买产品很着迷。
en: I'm fascinated by how companies develop under the market economy and how they persuade clients to buy products.

zh: 我是个值得信赖的人，总能和遇到的每个人相处融洽。
en: I am reliable and I always get on well with everyone I meet.

zh: 然后我就能朝着理想前进了。
en: Then I'll be on my way.

zh: 我知道你已经申请了我公司国际销售部销售顾问的职位......
en: I see you've applied for the position of sales consultant in our international sales department...

zh: 我将为我的国家鞠躬尽瘁，死而后已。
en: I will work heart and soul for my country to the day I draw my last breath.

zh: 知之者不如好之者，好之者不如乐之者。
en: Those who know it are not as those who love it; those who love it are not as those who find their joy in it.

zh: 一些社会学家正试图研究提高情商的可能性，特别是“人际技能”方面，例如：理解和沟通。
en: Some are trying to study the possibility of improving a person's EQ, especially in terms of "people skills", such as understanding and communication.

zh: 要在这个世界上出人头地，过快乐又成功的生活，必须要与他人相处融洽，能够以最好的方式理解和应对各种状况。
en: To get ahead in the world and lead a happy successful life requires getting on well with other people and being able to understand and react to situations in the best way possible.

zh: 在你祖父母生活的时代，人们有可能从毕业到退休都做同样的工作。
en: In your grandparents' time, there was the prospect of doing the same job from graduation until retirement.

zh: 随函附上本人简历一份。
en: I enclose a copy of my CV.

zh: 格林威治村是艺术人士聚集的地方，他们在那里寻找窗户朝北（阴面）且低租金的公寓。
en: Greenwich Village is a place where the art people came together, hunting for apartments with north-facing windows and low rents.

zh: 然而，她凝视着窗外，倒数着“十，九，八，七......”。
en: Instead, she stared out the window and counted backwards: "Ten, nine, eight, seven ..."

zh: 苏立即答道：“我从未听说过这么荒谬的话。”
en: "Oh, I never heard of such nonsense," quickly replied Sue.

zh: 但是约翰西决心已定。
en: But Johnsy's mind was made up:

zh: 40年来，他从未创作出他所渴望的杰作。
en: In 40 years he had never produced the masterpiece he longed to paint.

zh: 昨天早晨，看门人发现他无助地躺在床上，极度痛苦。
en: The janitor found him lying in bed helplessly with pain yesterday morning.

zh: 突然，我感到自己被迅速拉出水面；我听见有人说：“如果主人能抓住我的肩膀，主人会游得轻松很多。”
en: Suddenly I felt myself quickly drawn up to the surface of the sea; and I heard these words: "If master would be so good as to hold onto my shoulder, master would swim with much greater ease."

zh: 接着传来了沉重的开门声，两位水手出现了。
en: Then came the sound of a heavy door opening, and two seamen appeared.

zh: 我再也不会不把家当回事了。
en: I would never again take my home for granted.

zh: 他们的身体开始自行回暖！
en: Their bodies started to warm up on their own!

zh: 人的生命是有限的，可是，为人民服务是无限的，我要把有限的生命投入到无限的为人民服务之中去。
en: A person's life is limited but serving the people is limitless. I want to devote my limited life to serving the people limitlessly.

zh: 中中和华华是世界首对体细胞克隆猴。
en: Zhong Zhong and Hua Hua are the world's first pair of somatic cell cloned monkeys.

zh: 首只克隆猴“中中”于2017年11月27日在位于上海的中国科学院神经科学研究所、脑科学与智能技术卓越创新中心的非人灵长类平台诞生。
en: The first cloned monkey, 'Zhong Zhong', was born on November 27, 2017, at the Non-Human Primate Platform of the Institute of Neuroscience, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Center for Excellence in Brain Science and Intelligence Technology in Shanghai.

zh: 2017年12月5日，第二只克隆猴“华华”诞生。
en: On December 5, 2017, the second cloned monkey, 'Hua Hua', was born.

zh: 研究小组的负责人孙强博士说，他们全身心地投入到研究中，5年多来每周7天，每天24小时照看1000多只猴子。
en: Dr Sun Qiang, director of the research team, said they had been wholly devoted to the research and had been taking care of more than 1,000 monkeys 24 hours a day 7 days a week for more than 5 years.

zh: 这些（脑细胞）构成了神经细胞，神经细胞由电脉冲连接起来。
en: These make up nerve cells, which are connected by electrical impulses.

zh: 如果你的大脑能获得正确的养料，它就能保持最佳工作状态。
en: If your brain has the correct fuel, it can work at its best.

zh: 你可以做的一件事是减少吃垃圾食品。
en: One thing you can do is to cut down on junk food.

zh: Omega-3是一种多元不饱和脂肪酸，因其第一个双键位于第三个碳原子而得名，在深海鱼类和某些植物中含量比较高。
en: Omega-3 is a type of polyunsaturated fatty acid, named because its first double bond is located at the third carbon atom, and it is found in high concentrations in deep-sea fish and certain plants.

zh: 只有致死病例达到一定数量，某种疾病才会被正式定性为流行病。
en: Each disease can only officially be classified as an epidemic once a certain number of deaths have occurred from the disease.

zh: 吉姆·史密斯，美国加利福尼亚大学洛杉矶分校生态学与进化生物学系教授，对人畜共患传染病进行了大量的权威研究。
en: Jim Smith is a professor in the Department of Ecology and Evolutionary Biology at the University of California, Los Angeles, and has conducted extensive authoritative research on zoonotic infectious diseases.

zh: 刚刚熬过战争的家庭，现在又不得不埋葬死于疾病的亲人。
en: Families who had only just got through the war now had to bury their loved ones who had died of disease.

zh: 难以想象人们将怎样熬过这个冬天。
en: It is hard to see how people will get through the winter.

zh: 该病毒传染性极强，一些地区的存活率低至30%，也就是说很少有人能康复。
en: The virus was extremely infectious and the survival rate was as low as 30% in some areas, indicating that very few people got over it.

zh: 那么，萨姆，你觉得自己十年后会从事什么工作？
en: So, Sam, which job do you see yourself doing in ten years' time?

zh: 我希望自己能成为一名经理，负责一个团队成员。
en: I hope I'll be a manager and be responsible for a team of other members.

zh: 你和你的团队会做什么类型的工作？你会在什么类型的公司工作？
en: And what type of work would you and your team be doing? What type of company would you be working in?

zh: 我想在一家大公司的市场部工作。
en: I'd like to work in the marketing department of a big company.

zh: 我对公司在市场经济下如何发展以及如何说服客户购买产品非常着迷。
en: I'm fascinated by how companies develop under the market economy and how they persuade clients to buy products.

zh: 你能想到自己有哪些个人素质或技能适合这个工作吗？
en: Can you think of any personal qualities or skills you have which would make you suitable for that?

zh: 我有出色的沟通技巧。
en: I have excellent communication skills.

zh: 我可靠，并且总是能和遇到的每个人相处融洽。
en: I am reliable and I always get on well with everyone I meet.

zh: 现在我需要在学校努力学习——这样我就能获得在大学攻读商业或传播学学位所需的成绩和学分。
en: Now I need to work hard at school—so I get the grades and credits I need to do a degree in business or communication studies at a university.

zh: 然后我就能上路了。
en: Then I'll be on my way.

zh: 很高兴认识你，克里斯。
en: Nice to meet you, Chris.

zh: 现在......我看到你申请了我们国际销售部的销售顾问职位......
en: Now... I see you've applied for the position of sales consultant in our international sales department ...

zh: 自从大学毕业后，我已经在销售行业工作了四年。
en: I've been working in sales for four years since I left university.

zh: 太好了，是的，我在你的简历上看到了。
en: Brilliant, yes, I can see that on your CV here.

zh: 你大学时学的是什么专业来着？
en: And what was it that you studied at university again?

zh: 哦，是的，我现在看到了。
en: Oh yes, I can see it now.

zh: 你在剑桥大学获得了学士学位，在伦敦大学获得了硕士学位——这很令人印象深刻。
en: You got your bachelor's degree at the University of Cambridge and your master's at the University of London — that's impressive.

zh: 但你决定从事销售，而不是金融或与数学更密切相关的工作？
en: But you decided to go into sales rather than finance or something more closely linked to Maths?

zh: 是的，嗯，我热爱数学，对数字很着迷，但我也喜欢与人交流，并且我认为我有出色的沟通技巧。
en: Yes, well, I love Maths and I'm fascinated by numbers, but I also love communicating with people and I think I have excellent communication skills.

zh: 所以从事销售工作对我来说是完美的，因为它让我有机会同时做这两件事。
en: So working in sales is perfect for me because it gives me the chance to do both of those things.

zh: 而且，除此之外，你认为作为一名销售顾问，你最好的品质是什么？
en: And, apart from all of that, what do you think is your best quality as a sales consultant?

zh: 我会说我是一个可靠的人。
en: I would say that I'm reliable.

zh: 我知道每个人都这么说，但我确实是一个非常可靠的人，我不会让你失望的。
en: I know everyone says that, but I really am a very reliable person and I won't let you down.

zh: 我很快就要毕业了，我在选择一门能在未来找到好工作的大学课程上遇到了困难。
en: I'm leaving school soon, and I am having difficulty choosing a university course that will lead to a good job in the future.

zh: 嗯，寻找一门能帮助你找到工作的课程是好的，但我们无法准确预测未来的就业趋势。
en: Well, it's good to look for a course that will help you get a job, but we can't predict exactly what the job trends of the future will be.

zh: 许多常规的办公室或工厂工作很可能会消失，而在信息技术、生物技术和绿色技术等领域将会出现许多新型工作。
en: A lot of routine office or factory jobs will most likely disappear and there'll be many new types of jobs in areas such as information technology, bio-technology and green technology.

zh: 这意味着我们需要准备好新的技能和能力。
en: That means we need to get prepared with new skills and abilities.

zh: 你能告诉我更多关于这些的信息吗？
en: Can you tell me more about these?

zh: 嗯，首先，你需要具备学习新技能的能力。
en: Well, first, you need to have the ability to learn new skills.

zh: 过去，人们为一份工作接受培训，然后终生从事这份工作。
en: In the past, people were trained for one job and then stayed in it for their whole lives.

zh: 未来，人们很可能在多年里从事四到五份不同的工作。
en: In the future, people will probably have four or five different jobs over many years.

zh: 因此，终身学习是最重要的技能。
en: So learning throughout life is the most important skill.

zh: 我还需要哪些其他技能？
en: What other skills will I need?

zh: 嗯，使用互联网、学习新的IT技能以及跟上技术发展将非常重要。
en: Well, using the Internet, learning new IT skills and keeping up with technology developments will be very important.

zh: 这些将为你提供未来找工作所需的工具。
en: These will give you the tools you need to find work in the future.

zh: 你认为它们会重要吗？
en: Do you think they will be important?

zh: 第二语言现在当然很重要，并且将继续如此。
en: A second language is certainly important now and will continue to be so.

zh: 随着工作环境变得更加国际化，人们为工作而周游世界，理解语言和文化将对有效沟通大有帮助。
en: As work environments become more international and people travel around the world for work, understanding languages and cultures will help greatly in effective communication.

zh: 中文、西班牙语和阿拉伯语是越来越受欢迎的学习语言。
en: Chinese, Spanish and Arabic are languages that are becoming more popular to learn.

zh: 我们还需要哪些可以应用于不同工作的其他技能吗？
en: Are there any other skills we will need that can be applied to different jobs?

zh: 是的，有几项重要的通用技能。
en: Yes, there are several important broad skills.

zh: 批判性思维是一个需要发展的关键领域。
en: Critical thinking is a key area to develop.

zh: 这是思考和评估信息的能力，而不仅仅是学习和记忆它。
en: This is the ability to consider and evaluate information rather than just learn and remember it.

zh: 另一项重要的技能是创造力。
en: Another important skill is creativity.

zh: 随着许多旧的、常规的工作消失或被人工智能取代，人们将被要求做更具创造性的工作，提出新想法，并思考如何改进现有的流程和活动。
en: As many old, routine jobs disappear or are done by AI, people will be required to do more creative work, coming up with new ideas and thinking about how to improve existing processes and activities.

zh: 非常感谢，麦克杜格尔先生。
en: Thanks very much, Mr McDougall.

zh: 我认为我应该去上大学并获得学位。
en: I think I should go to university and get a degree.

zh: 那样的话，我毕业时肯定能找到工作。
en: That way I'll be sure to get a job when I graduate.

zh: 除非你有具体想学的东西，否则上大学还有其他选择。
en: Unless you have something specific you want to study, there are alternatives to going to university.

zh: 上大学并不能保证找到工作。
en: Going to university does not guarantee a job.

zh: 我不确定我同意这一点。
en: I'm not sure I agree with that.

zh: 我父母说大学学位是好工作的保证。
en: My parents say that a university degree is a guarantee of a good job.

zh: 世界正在发生巨大的变化。
en: There is a great change going on in the world.

zh: 很可能你不会只做一份工作。
en: Probably you will not stay in one job.

zh: 此外，想想人工智能是如何改变就业市场的。
en: Also, just think about how AI is changing the job market.

zh: 不要花大量时间去学习未来人工智能就能做的事情。
en: Don't spend a lot of time studying for something that AI will be able to do in the future.

zh: 啊，说得好。
en: Ah, that's a good point.

zh: 我知道人工智能将承担很多现在人们做的工作。
en: I know AI will be doing a lot of the jobs people do now.

zh: 因此，人们将需要不同的技能来应对未来的就业市场。
en: So people will need different skills to deal with the job market of the future.

zh: 是的，我认为跟上所有最新的程序、应用程序和信息技术将非常重要。
en: Yes, I suppose staying up to date with all the latest programmes, apps and information technology will be very important.

zh: 是的。我完全同意你的看法。
en: Yes. I couldn't agree with you more.

zh: 这现在已经发生了，并且未来会更加普遍。
en: It's already happening now and will happen even more in the future.

zh: 人们不再需要去办公室了——他们可以在任何地方工作，并与世界各地的人保持联系。
en: People don't need to go into an office anymore — they can work from anywhere and be in touch with people all over the world.

zh: 这意味着商业将更加国际化。
en: This means that business will be more international.

zh: 我想我们必须一直持续学习。
en: I suppose we have to continue learning all the time.

zh: 这一点我同意你。
en: I agree with you there.

zh: 我们还需要灵活变通，并成为良好的沟通者。
en: We also need to be flexible and be good communicators.

zh: 总而言之，我想你可以说我们需要具备良好的计算机技能，有积极性、创造力、勤奋、乐于学习并且能够良好地沟通！
en: To sum it up, I suppose you could say we need to have good computer skills, be motivated, creative, hard-working, ready to learn and be able to communicate well!

zh: 我们都需要不断发展和适应一个变化的世界，不是吗？
en: We all need to keep developing and adapting to a changing world, don't we?

zh: 我看你读很多书。
en: I see that you read a lot.

zh: 你经常读什么书？
en: What books do you often read?

zh: 我读各种各样的书。
en: I read a wide variety of books.

zh: 在所有这些书中，我最喜欢读文学作品，比如科幻小说、侦探故事、小说和诗歌。
en: Among all these books, I like reading literary works the most, such as science fiction, detective stories, novels and poetry.

zh: 哇！你是说你喜欢侦探故事？
en: Wow! You mean you like detective stories?

zh: 我觉得侦探故事令人兴奋且刺激。
en: I find detective stories exciting and stimulating.

zh: 我喜欢其中的谜团。
en: I love the mysteries in them.

zh: 我总是尝试自己解开谜题。
en: I always try to solve the riddles myself.

zh: 我从未读过任何侦探故事。
en: I've never read any detective stories.

zh: 如果我想开始读这类故事，你有什么建议吗？
en: If I want to start reading this kind of stories, do you have any suggestion?

zh: 我建议你从读《月亮宝石》开始。
en: I suggest you read The Moonstone as a start.

zh: 它写于19世纪。
en: It's written in the 19th century.

zh: 事实上，它是有史以来第一部英语侦探小说！
en: In fact, it's the first-ever English detective novel!

zh: 嗯，它会不会很无聊？
en: Well, isn't it going to be boring?

zh: 我的意思是，它发生在很久以前。
en: I mean, it takes place such a long time ago.

zh: 哦不，它有一个好情节，而且背景很有趣！
en: Oh no, it has a good plot, and the setting is interesting!

zh: 历史小说可以教你很多关于不同时代和文化的知识。
en: Historical novels can teach you a lot about different eras and cultures.

zh: 好的。我会试一试。
en: OK. I'll have a try.

zh: 我家里有一本。
en: I have a copy at home.

zh: 我明天可以带给你。
en: I can bring it to you tomorrow.

zh: 你好，这是我第一次来这个图书馆。
en: Hello, this is the first time that I have come to this library.

zh: 我能问一些信息吗？
en: Can I ask for some information?

zh: 我正在做一个比较英汉诗歌的项目。
en: I'm doing a project on comparing English and Chinese poetry.

zh: 你们有英文版的中国诗歌书籍吗？
en: Do you have any books of Chinese poetry in English?

zh: 我们有大量不同语言的文学作品收藏。
en: We have big collections of literary works in different languages.

zh: 请这边走......它们在这里，在这个书架上。
en: Just come this way ... Here they are, on this shelf.

zh: 如果我想借一本书，需要做什么？
en: What do I need to do if I want to take a book out?

zh: 你只需要填写并提交一张借书证的登记表。
en: You just need to fill in and submit a registration form for a library card.

zh: 然后，你所要做的就是扫描你的借书证，并且扫描书上的号码。
en: Then, all you have to do is to scan your library card, and also scan the number on the book.

zh: 然后你就可以把书借出去了。
en: Then you may take the book out.

zh: 登记表在那边的那张桌子上。
en: The registration form is over there on that desk.

zh: 如今，我的许多朋友都有电子阅读器或者在网上读文章，而不是读真正的书；但我还是习惯手里拿着书。
en: Nowadays, many of my friends have e-readers or read articles online instead of reading real books; but I'm still used to holding books in my hands.

zh: 我明白你的意思。
en: I know what you mean.

zh: 很多人仍然喜欢读一本真正的书——至少在某些时候是这样！
en: Many people still like to read a real book — at least some of the time, anyway!

zh: 我想我知道这是谁的树林。
en: Whose woods these are I think I know.

zh: 不过他的房子在村子里；
en: His house is in the village though;

zh: 他不会看见我停在这里
en: He will not see me stopping here

zh: 观看他的树林积满白雪。
en: To watch his woods fill up with snow.

zh: 我的小马一定觉得奇怪
en: My little horse must think it queer

zh: 停在附近没有农舍的地方
en: To stop without a farmhouse near

zh: 在树林和冰封的湖泊之间
en: Between the woods and frozen lake

zh: 在一年中最漆黑的夜晚。
en: The darkest evening of the year.

zh: 他摇响了他的马具铃铛
en: He gives his harness bells a shake

zh: 想问问是否有什么错误。
en: To ask if there is some mistake.

zh: 唯一其他的声音是轻风与柔雪的拂扫。
en: The only other sound's the sweep of easy wind and downy flake.

zh: 树林可爱、幽暗而深邃，
en: The woods are lovely, dark and deep,

zh: 但我还有诺言要遵守，
en: But I have promises to keep,

zh: 入睡前还有很长的路要走，
en: And miles to go before I sleep,

zh: 入睡前还有很长的路要走。
en: And miles to go before I sleep.

zh: 我像一朵云独自漫游，
en: I wandered lonely as a cloud,

zh: 高高飘过溪谷和山丘，
en: That floats on high o'er vales and hills,

zh: 忽然间我看到一群，
en: When all at once I saw a crowd,

zh: 一大片金色的水仙花；
en: A host, of golden daffodils;

zh: 在湖边，在树下，
en: Beside the lake, beneath the trees,

zh: 在微风中摇曳起舞。
en: Fluttering and dancing in the breeze.

zh: 连绵不断如闪耀的繁星，
en: Continuous as the stars that shine,

zh: 在银河中闪烁，
en: And twinkle on the milky way,

zh: 它们延伸成无尽的队列，
en: They stretched in never-ending line,

zh: 沿着海湾的边缘；
en: Along the margin of a bay;

zh: 我一眼望去看到成千上万，
en: Ten thousand saw I at a glance,

zh: 欢快地舞动它们的头。
en: Tossing their heads in sprightly dance.

zh: 旁边的波浪起舞；但它们
en: The waves beside them danced; but they

zh: 在欢乐中胜过闪亮的波浪：
en: Outdid the sparkling waves in glee:

zh: 诗人不能不感到快乐，
en: A poet could not but be gay,

zh: 在如此欢乐的陪伴中：
en: In such a jocund company:

zh: 我凝视——再凝视——却很少想到，
en: I gazed — and gazed — but little thought,

zh: 这景象给我带来了怎样的财富：
en: What wealth the show to me had brought:

zh: 因为常常，当我躺在沙发上，
en: For oft, when on my couch I lie,

zh: 在空虚或沉思的情绪中，
en: In vacant or in pensive mood,

zh: 它们闪现在内心的眼睛上，
en: They flash upon that inward eye,

zh: 那是孤独的幸福；
en: Which is the bliss of solitude;

zh: 然后我的心充满喜悦，
en: And then my heart with pleasure fills,

zh: 并与水仙花共舞。
en: And dances with the daffodils.

zh: 我刚刚在电视上看田径比赛。
en: I've just been watching athletics on television.

zh: 我对运动员的速度和力量感到惊讶。
en: I'm amazed at the speed and strength of the athletes.

zh: 人体能够做很多事情，比如跳得高、跑得快。
en: The human body is capable of doing many things, such as jumping high, running very fast.

zh: 我喜欢看体操比赛。
en: I love watching gymnastics competitions.

zh: 人类的思想也很神奇——它能够做决策、记忆事实、推理以及做许多其他事情。
en: The human mind is amazing, too — the human mind is capable of making decisions, memorising facts, reasoning and doing so many other things.

zh: 我们也用我们的思想去创造和创新。
en: We use our minds to create and innovate as well.

zh: 我真的很钦佩那些用自己的智慧为人类谋福祉的人。
en: I really admire people who use their minds for the betterment of mankind.

zh: 例如，医学研究人员进行研究以寻找疾病的治疗方法。
en: For example, medical researchers conduct research to find cures for diseases.

zh: 他们也通过开发疫苗来寻找预防疾病的方法。
en: They also look for ways to prevent diseases by developing vaccines.

zh: 人类的思想和身体所能做到的难道不令人惊叹吗？
en: Isn't it amazing what the human mind and body are capable of?

zh: 米勒豪斯博士，感谢您今天抽空与我们交谈。
en: Thank you for taking the time to talk to us today, Dr. Millhouse.

zh: 您一直在研究人体在极端条件下的反应。
en: You've been doing some research into how the human body reacts in extreme conditions.

zh: 您能告诉我们您发现了什么吗？
en: Could you tell us what you've found out?

zh: 首先，我们研究了人类在极热和极冷气候下的恢复力。
en: First of all, we looked at human resilience in hot and cold extreme climates.

zh: 我们训练实验参与者做特殊的呼吸练习，然后他们在我们实验室的极低温度下进行这些练习。
en: We trained the experiment's participants to do special breathing exercises and then they did them in extremely low temperatures in our lab.

zh: 在他们进行了大约二十分钟的呼吸练习后，我们注意到一个非常有趣的现象——他们的身体开始自行发热！
en: After they'd been doing the breathing exercises for about twenty minutes, we noticed something very interesting — their bodies started to warm up on their own!

zh: 当参与者开始感到疲倦时，他们就不再正确地做练习了。
en: When the participants started to feel tired, they stopped doing the exercises properly.

zh: 然后他们的体温又开始下降。
en: Then their body temperature started to fall again.

zh: 好的，所以您的意思是人们能够控制自己身体的冷热？
en: OK, so what you're saying is that people are able to control how hot or cold their bodies are?

zh: 我们看到了与那些涉及使用兴奋剂的运动员身上类似的效果。
en: We saw similar effects to those we see in the bodies of athletes involved in doping.

zh: 但我们需要做更多的研究来确切地弄清楚这一切是如何运作的。
en: But we need to do some more research to find out how this all works exactly.

zh: 非常感谢您，米勒豪斯博士。
en: Thank you very much, Dr. Millhouse.

zh: 今天与您交谈非常有趣。
en: It's been very interesting to talk to you today.

zh: 好的，今天讲座的主题是“大脑”。
en: Right, the topic of today's lecture is "The Brain".

zh: 嗯，大脑是一个绝对神奇的器官。
en: Well, the brain is an absolutely amazing organ.

zh: 它由灰质和白质组成。
en: It consists of grey and white matter.

zh: 它的重量仅略超一公斤——想象一下，就像一公斤土豆。
en: It weighs just over a kilo — just imagine that, like a kilo of potatoes.

zh: 但这是非常特殊的一公斤。
en: But this is a very special kilo.

zh: 它消耗身体百分之二十的能量，并且包含超过一千亿个细胞。
en: It uses twenty percent of the body's energy and it contains over one hundred billion cells.

zh: 这些构成了神经细胞，它们通过电脉冲连接。
en: These make up nerve cells, which are connected by electrical impulses.

zh: 大脑使用电脉冲发送信息，其方式与计算机工作方式相同。
en: The brain sends messages using electrical impulses, the same way as a computer works.

zh: 但一个大脑中可能的连接比宇宙中的原子还要多。
en: But there are more possible connections in one brain than there are atoms in the universe.

zh: 再想一想。
en: Just think about that again.

zh: 你大脑中可能的连接比宇宙中的原子还要多。
en: There are more possible connections in your brain than there are atoms in the universe.

zh: 这让普通的电脑看起来很简单，不是吗？
en: Makes a normal computer look simple, doesn't it?

zh: 那么，对于这个叫做“大脑”的器官，我们了解些什么？
en: So, what do we know about this organ called "the brain"?

zh: 研究大脑的科学家在过去十年里对它有了很多了解。
en: Scientists who study the brain have learnt a lot about it in the last ten years.

zh: 就像探索世界的探险家一样，他们发现大脑中有不同的区域，这些区域负责不同的功能。
en: Like explorers exploring the world, they've found that there are different areas in the brain and these areas are responsible for different functions.

zh: 我们现在知道大脑的哪些部分与我们身体的不同部位以及来自这些部位的感觉相关。
en: We now know which parts of the brain are related to different parts of our body and feelings from them.

zh: 一个很好的例子可以在那些在事故中失去手臂或腿的人身上看到。
en: A good example of this can be seen in people who have lost an arm or a leg in an accident.

zh: 尽管他们没有手臂或腿，他们仍然能感觉到来自那里的感觉，比如疼痛。
en: Though they don't have an arm or a leg, they can still have feelings from it, like pain.

zh: 你也有不同的区域负责不同种类的思考。
en: You also have different areas for different kinds of thinking.

zh: 例如，你有一个区域负责你的第一语言，另一个区域负责你长大后学习的外语。
en: For example, you have one area for your first language and another area for a foreign language that you learn when you are older.

zh: 但比定位大脑不同区域更有趣的是......
en: But even more interesting than locating different areas of the brain is ...

zh: 早上好，欢迎来到《健康生活》。
en: Good morning and welcome to Healthy Living.

zh: 今天早上我们在这里讨论大脑。
en: This morning we're here to discuss the brain.

zh: 这位是金教授，一位大脑专家。
en: This is Professor Kim, a brain expert.

zh: 谢谢邀请我。
en: Thank you for having me.

zh: 现在让我们接听第一位来电者。
en: Let's turn to our first caller now.

zh: 你想问金教授什么问题？
en: What would you like to ask Professor Kim?

zh: 是的，我注意到我的记忆力不如以前了，而且我读过关于大脑锻炼的文章——你知道，填字游戏、游戏、测验之类的东西。
en: Yes, I've noticed that my memory isn't as good as it used to be, and I've read about brain exercises — you know, crossword puzzles, games, quizzes, that kind of thing.

zh: 但它们真的有效吗？
en: But do they really work?

zh: 您有什么建议，金教授？
en: What's your advice, Professor Kim?

zh: 嗯，答案既是肯定的也是否定的。
en: Well, the answer is yes and no.

zh: 谜题和游戏对我们保持大脑活跃有好处。
en: Puzzles and games are good for us to keep our brain active.

zh: 然而，要真正锻炼大脑，你需要挑战它。
en: However, to really exercise the brain, you need to challenge it.

zh: 这意味着做一些新的事情，一些你也许不擅长的事情。
en: That means doing something new, something that perhaps you are not good at.

zh: 这能保持大脑的灵活性并有助于改善记忆力。
en: This keeps the brain flexible and helps improve memory.

zh: 那么您建议哪些类型的活动呢？
en: So what kinds of activities do you suggest?

zh: 你可以尝试学习一门新语言、学习跳舞或学习绘画......任何能让你走出舒适区的事情。
en: You could try learning a new language, learning to dance or learning to paint ... anything that takes you outside your comfort zone.

zh: 太好了。我们请下一位来电者好吗？
en: Great. Can we have our next caller, please?

zh: 嗨，约翰，请问你的问题是什么？
en: Hi, John, what's your question, please?

zh: 哦，嗨。我下个月有一些重要的考试，我只是想知道是否有某些食物对大脑有益。
en: Oh, hi. I've got some big exams coming up next month, and I was just wondering if there are certain foods that are good for the brain.

zh: 你的饮食绝对可以改善你的大脑健康。
en: Your diet can definitely improve your brain health.

zh: 如果你的大脑有正确的燃料，它就能以最佳状态工作。
en: If your brain has the correct fuel, it can work at its best.

zh: 你可以做的一件事是减少垃圾食品的摄入。
en: One thing you can do is to cut down on junk food.

zh: 对大脑有益的食物是天然食物：水果、蔬菜等等。
en: Foods that help the brain are natural foods: fruit, vegetables, and so on.

zh: 增加欧米伽-3脂肪的摄入量可能也是个好主意，这是一种重要的健脑食品。
en: It might also be a good idea to increase your intake of Omega-3 fats, which are an important brain food.

zh: 这些存在于多脂鱼和鸡蛋等食物中。
en: These are found in food like oily fish and eggs.

zh: 最后，我们能听听贝拉的吗？
en: And finally, can we hear from Bella?

zh: 我的问题是关于体育锻炼的。
en: My question is about physical exercise.

zh: 有没有什么特别的运动可以改善我大脑的健康？
en: Is there any particular kind of exercise that can improve the health of my brain?

zh: 我建议你做对心脏有益的运动，比如散步和跑步。
en: I advise you to do exercise that is good for your heart, such as walking and running.

zh: 这些运动增加了血液中的氧气量，这对大脑也有好处。
en: These kinds of exercise increase the amount of oxygen in the blood, which is also good for the brain.

zh: 休息后请继续收听......
en: Join us after the break ...
